[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#containers)

Containers


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Containers are elements that can contain other elements. They are used to keep groups of elements in one place, control parts of their responsive settings and opacity, and for navigation.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#group)

Group


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Groups are elements that contain other elements. A group has two roles. First, a group gathers elements from a visual perspective, and all the elements in that group move together. It also has a semantic significance. A group can have data injected into it, and the elements inside the group can refer to the Parent group's thing. For example, you can 'display' a user in a group, and all elements inside that group will be able to display the 'Parent group's user's email.' Use this functionality when you want some areas of the app's page to display items relative to a dynamic entry.

Our Academy quick tip on how to group elements together

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#container-layout)

Container layout

A Group's container layout defines it's and it's child elements responsive behavior. See more in [Container Layout Types.](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/container-layout-types)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#type-of-content)

Type of content

This allows you to select the type of thing this container displays. Most elements that have this property also have a Data source property, which lets you select the displayed thing or list of things. The value of the data source must match the selected type of content. Let's look at an example of a map with hotels marked. When you click a marker, a popup displays with the name of the hotel. The Type of content would be 'Hotel' and the Data source would be 'Map's current marker.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#data-source)

Data source

Define the thing or list of things that should be displayed in this container. For example, in groups or popups, only a single thing will display, but for repeating groups a list of things will display. Child elements can access the Data source from their parent. If the parent contains a list of things, the child has access to the individual list items. A common scenario for groups is to display something, like the current user, and then have the group contain the inputs that modify the user's properties, such as text and images that display information about the user. The Type of content determines what kind of thing you are allowed to set in the Data source field. If the type of thing does not match the Data source, it will be marked as an issue, and you must change one or the other to match. The Data source can come from anywhere in Bubble. For example, it could be the result of a search, a custom or built-in Data source, or it could be dynamic and come from the user. The Data source will update when its source changes. For example, if the search result changes because a user modified some data used in the search constraints, the displayed list will automatically change. For additional flexibility, you can update the Data source in the Conditional Formatting Tab in the Property Editor. Finally, the Data source property can be modified by workflows. Changes made by actions override whatever you specify as the Data source directly or as a conditional state until you run a reset action, which then restores the original setting.

**Note:** As mentioned, Groups can have data assigned to them (e.g. 'this group should show data about user X'). When the data assigned to a group changes, the group will reset all the input fields within itself.

This is useful when the input fields are supposed to change depending on the group's data. But, it can also cause confusion, for example, when a group's data depends on an input that's inside that group!

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#collapse-when-hidden)

Collapse when hidden

By default, hiding an element leaves a blank space on the page, exposing whatever is underneath it. When you select this option, however, the elements below the hidden element are pulled upward to fill the space.

**Tip:** A common pattern for a messaging UI involves a Repeating Group where cells have two Groups (Sender & Receiver) that display the text sent by each respective party. When the message belongs to the Sender, the Receiver group is hidden and vice versa.

Because of a race condition between evaluating conditional visibility of groups in Repeating Group cells and evaluating cell height, blank spaces may appear when switching between the two groups when a new message is sent.

To minimize the risk of this happening, consider enabling "Collapse this element's height when hidden" on both Groups.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#animate-the-collapse-operation)

Animate the collapse operation

This option is only visible if 'Collapse this element's height when hidden' is selected. By default, elements collapse and expand instantly, but this option animates with either Fade In/Out or Slide Up/Down.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#animation-style)

Animation style

This option allows you to choose whether an element's animation will Fade In/Out or Slide Up/Down.

**Note:** Animation Style is not currently supported for use on a group in situations where a repeating group's underlying data is changing. In these situations, consider relying on the repeating group's built-in updating instead of adding an animation.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#start-edit-workflow)

Start/Edit workflow

Click this button to create a new workflow triggered by this element. The type of event depends on the element and is generally the most common event. For example, buttons will create a 'When this element is clicked...' event. If a workflow already exists, this takes you to the existing workflow in the Workflow Tab. To create a different type of event or create a second event, go to the Workflow Tab and click the 'Click here to add an event...' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#this-element-isnt-clickable)

This element isn't clickable

When checked, the element will not trigger a workflow when clicked nor will the user's cursor change to a pointer when they hover over the element. In addition, the item will display according to any specified conditional formatting for the 'isn't clickable' state. This can be used to stop a user from moving forward in a flow until some condition is true. For example, we might disable a specific action until the user is logged in. 'This input should not be empty' can also be used to prevent the user from clicking.

**Tip:** Conditionally changing the borders of a Group will not resize the content in the group, which can lead to undesirable formatting. Consider using two groups (one without a border), and toggling between the two if this behavior is desired.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#repeating-group)

Repeating Group


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For a more in-depth look at repeating groups, read our dedicated Manual article on the subject:

Article: [Repeating groups](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements/web-app/containers/repeating-groups)

A repeating group displays lists of things, either coming from the application database or from APIs. Design the first cell, and the remaining cells will follow that design.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#type-of-content-1)

Type of content

This allows you to select the type of thing this container displays. Most elements that have this property also have a Data source property, which lets you select the displayed thing or list of things. The value of the data source must match the selected type of content. Let's look at an example of a map with hotels marked. When you click a marker, a popup displays with the name of the hotel. The Type of content would be 'Hotel' and the Data source would be 'Map's current marker.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#data-source-1)

Data source

This is the list of things to display in the group. It can either be

*   The result of a search using 'Do a search for,'
    
*   The content of a field whose type is list of things, or
    
*   The result of an API call through the 'Get data from an external API.'
    

Once this is set, each cell will display one thing, and the elements drawn inside the cell will be able to refer to the 'Current cell's thing.'

**Note:** Avoid the situation where you automatically change the data source of a repeating group based on a condition that references the repeating group itself! This will cause an infinite loop and the repeating group will not behave as you expect.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#layout-settings)

Layout settings

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#fixed-of-rows-or-columns)

Fixed # of rows or columns

Check fixed number of rows or column if you'd like to display a fixed number of rows or columns in run mode, regardless of the screen size or number of entries in the List. Check both boxes to recreate the legacy Fixed number of cells repeating group layout.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#undefined-1)

![Screen Shot 2021-09-09 at 5.32.01 PM.png](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcodahosted.io%2Fdocs%2F94P3_UlyvF%2Fblobs%2Fbl-lQ0pq6tD_w%2Fe9baddc9a97d2c59b062c645b74896d1f93053a78972894521903a8ac2bf467ced945fe1bd6bc814e2868f5f634de9522d135c3ff8e74cc90622a112f407d2aef60c267e03cd82cdf99b6e0ff7ce24a716a41ee9865ab9397b7c4eb85f9c38a749dcfaa1&width=300&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=3c5a019&sv=2)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#set-fixed-number-of-rows)

Set fixed number of rows

Check this box to have the repeating group show a fixed number of rows. Cell height will be determined by the height of the overall repeating group container divided by the number of rows specified. Use the show next / show previous workflow actions to paginate the table and show more data.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#rows)

Rows

Set the number of rows you’d like to display.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#set-fixed-number-of-columns)

Set fixed number of columns

Check this box to have the repeating group show a fixed number of columns. Cell width will be determined by the width of the overall repeating group container divided by the number of columns specified. Use the show next / how previous workflow actions to show more data.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#columns)

Columns

Set the number of columns you’d like to display.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#show-partial-list)

Show partial list on last page if needed

Check this box to show a less than specified number of rows or columns on the last page of a paginated table if needed.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#dynamic-of-rows-or-columns)

**Dynamic # of rows or columns**

Uncheck fixed number of rows or column if you'd like to display a dynamic number of rows or columns in run mode - i.e. to recreate legacy vertical scrolling or horizontal scrolling repeating group layouts.

Scrollbars, especially when in repeating groups, can sometimes cause elements to appear slightly different than designed on certain browsers and devices. This is because Bubble relies on the end-user's operating system and browser to determine how a scrollbar should look.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#undefined-2)

![Screen Shot 2021-09-09 at 5.32.16 PM.png](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcodahosted.io%2Fdocs%2F94P3_UlyvF%2Fblobs%2Fbl-nJakF1OUBG%2Fdf66b8d12f449aee9fe948910dbd5bd1b49f9204dab1f7e762b0dcdb31dbdf1e1cff72e48b6e9ee4a9a9ae03b2bb910d7654b2dbbfb91285e45214a9fbf87f883f0f49ac62c0cf12a27e05a82290cf3f62fdf3489f54fb91640fcc097fca8ceb3a298d13&width=300&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=4577778b&sv=2)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#min-height-of-row)

Min height of row

Specify a minimum Repeating Group row height. The Repeating Group will default to filling as many cells as the data allows at the minimum height, and then grow in size evenly to fill the remaining space.

For example, if you have a repeating group with a height of 440px, a min height of 100px, and 5 entries in your List, the repeating group in run-mode will initially show 4 cells at a height of 110px each.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#stretch-rows-to-fit)

Stretch rows to fill vertical space

If [Set fixed number of rows](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#set-fixed-number-of-rows)
 is unchecked, you can choose whether to stretch all rows to fill the available vertical space. If the rows at their minimum height do not fill the space, each row will expand to match the tallest content, making all rows equal in height.

If this setting is checked, the Display items as masonry grid option will not be available since that setting requires elements to fit height to their content.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#min-width-of-column)

Min width of column

Specify a minimum Repeating Group column width. The Repeating Group will default to filling as many cells as the Repeating Group Container width allows at the minimum cell width, and then grow in size evenly to fill the remaining space.

For example, if you have a repeating group with a width of 440px, a min width of 200px, and 10 entries, the repeating group in run-mode will initially show a repeating group with 2 columns at a width of 220px each.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#stretch-columns-to-fit)

Stretch columns to fill horizontal space

If [Set fixed number of columns](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#set-fixed-number-of-columns)
 is unchecked, choose whether to stretch columns to fill horizontal space. If columns at min width aren't wide enough to fill horizontal space, each column will stretch in width to do so.

If this setting is unchecked, the amount of data that is loaded will not impact the number of columns rendered on the page. For example, if 6 columns are able to fit within the Repeating Group container, but there are only two pieces of data in the List, 6 columns will still be rendered. On the other hand, if this setting is checked, the two columns with data will stretch to fit the amount of space available.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#scroll-direction)

Scroll direction

If [Set fixed number of rows](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#set-fixed-number-of-rows)
 and [Set fixed number of columns](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#set-fixed-number-of-columns)
 are both unchecked, choose the direction you’d like the repeating group to scroll.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#scroll-vertical)

Vertical

Select this option if you'd like your repeating group to scroll vertically if the amount of rows takes up more space than the parent container allows. This is usually the most common option.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#scroll-horizontal)

Horizontal

Select this option if you'd like your repeating group to scroll horizontally if the amount of rows takes up more space than the parent container allows.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#wrapped-horizontally)

Wrapped horizontally

Select this option if you'd like the items in your repeating group to wrap to a new row if the number of items takes up more space than the parent container allows. Use this setting if you'd like to create a "tag" like list where columns fit width to their content and do not need to be evenly sized.

This option has access to [Row gap](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/responsive-properties#row-gap-px)
 and [Column gap](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/responsive-properties#column-gap-px)
 settings.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#reverse-scroll-direction)

Reverse scroll direction

Check this box to show the first item in a repeating group on the bottom instead of the top. Adding items to the front of the list will show on the bottom, while scrolling upwards will show the end of the list. This option requires a fixed height, the scroll direction to be “vertical” or to have a fixed number of columns, and no fixed number of rows.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#show-all-items-immediately)

Show all items immediately

Check this box to load all of the data right away. This is similar to legacy Full List repeating group behavior.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#display-items-as-masonry-grid)

Display items as masonry grid

Check this box to display the data in your repeating group as a "pinterest style" grid. The masonry grid setting allows each cell to take up only as much space as needed in each column's row, as opposed to requiring a fixed row height across all columns like normal repeating group tables. This setting is only available when [Set fixed number of rows](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#set-fixed-number-of-rows)
 is unchecked.

If this setting is checked, the [Stretch rows to fill vertical space](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#stretch-rows-to-fit)
 option will not be available since this setting requires elements to fit height to their content.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#container-layout-type)

Container layout type

A repeating group cell is a container as well and can have one of the following 4 container layout types: [Fixed](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/container-layout-types#fixed)
, [Align to parent](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/container-layout-types#align-to-parent)
, [Row](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/container-layout-types#row)
, and [Column](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/container-layout-types#column)
. Select the appropriate container layout type depending on what child element behavior you want.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#separator-style)

Separator Style

Select the type of border to display between the cells. Choose from None or a stylized line, such as Solid, Dashed, and Double.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#separator-width)

Separator Width

Enter the width to apply to the separator in pixels. Set a width even with a separator of None. In this case, the cells will have some empty space between them.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#separator-color)

Separator Color

Select the separator color.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#start-edit-workflow-1)

Start/Edit workflow

Click this button to create a new workflow triggered by this element. The type of event depends on the element and is generally the most common event. For example, buttons will create a 'When this element is clicked...' event. If a workflow already exists, this takes you to the existing workflow in the Workflow Tab. To create a different type of event or create a second event, go to the Workflow Tab and click the 'Click here to add an event...' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#this-element-isnt-clickable-1)

This element isn't clickable

When checked, the element will not trigger a workflow when clicked nor will the user's cursor change to a pointer when they hover over the element. In addition, the item will display according to any specified conditional formatting for the 'isn't clickable' state. This can be used to stop a user from moving forward in a flow until some condition is true. For example, we might disable a specific action until the user is logged in. 'This input should not be empty' can also be used to prevent the user from clicking.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#table-element)

Table element


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For a more in-depth look at the table element, read our dedicated Manual article on the subject:

Article: [The table element](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements/web-app/containers/table-elements)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#table-element-1)

Table element

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#type-of-content-2)

Type of content

This allows you to select the data type this container displays, such as User.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#data-source-2)

Data source

The table element expects a list of things to be returned. Each cell of the table element’s repeating rows can then refer to the Current cell’s thing.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#table-direction)

Table direction

This property determines whether the content of the table is loaded vertically (along the Y-axis) or horizontally (along the X-axis). The setting affects both static and repeating rows/columns)

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#vertical-and-horizontal-separators)

Vertical and horizontal separators

This property lets you enable and set the styling options for the vertical and horizontal separators. Note that these settings can be affected by the [_Table direction_](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#table-direction)
 setting.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#static-rows-columns)

Static rows/columns

Note: Whether the settings below are applied to rows or columns depends on the Table orientation setting on the table element.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#make-this-row-column-sticky)

Make this row/column sticky

This property enables the row to be fixed at the top of the element, ensuring it stays visible as you scroll through the rows below. This is particularly useful for keeping headers or key information in sight while navigating extensive data sets.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#repeating-rows-columns)

Repeating rows/columns

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#set-fixed-number-of-repeating-rows-columns)

Set fixed number of repeating rows/columns

This property instructs Bubble to only display a set number of repeating rows in the table. Checking it will reveal the setting below to set the number of rows.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#row-column-count)

Row/column count

This property sets the maximum number of rows that you want to display. If the data source contains a list that is shorter than the maximum number of rows set, Bubble will only display the number of rows equal to the length of the list.

When working with the table element, the **contextual menu** offers a lot of different options for managing table rows and columns. You can read more about this in the reference entry for the contextual menu.

Working with the table element introduces a lot of new options to Bubble's **contextual menu**. You can see a list of all of these options in the contextual menu reference entry.

Reference: [Contextual menu](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/top-and-context-menu-options#cut-column)
 (table element entries)

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#popup)

Popup


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Popups are hidden from the user until you display them from the Workflow Tab. When they are visible, they are fixed position in the center of the viewport. If the popup is longer than the viewport, the user will be able to scroll to see the full content. If the popup is shorter than the viewport, the user will not be able to scroll.

Our Academy quick tip on how to use popups

**Tip:** If the popup is longer than the viewport, scrolling will be enabled, and scroll distance is tied to the length of the page in the background. If you have a very long page, the user will be able to continue scrolling the entire length of the background page, potentially past the point where the popup is no longer visible.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#type-of-content-3)

Type of content

This allows you to select the type of thing this container displays. Most elements that have this property also have a Data source property, which lets you select the displayed thing or list of things. The value of the data source must match the selected type of content. Let's look at an example of a map with hotels marked. When you click a marker, a popup displays with the name of the hotel. The Type of content would be 'Hotel' and the Data source would be 'Map's current marker.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#data-source-3)

Data source

Define the thing or list of things that should be displayed in this container. For example, in groups or popups, only a single thing will display, but for repeating groups a list of things will display. Child elements can access the Data source from their parent. If the parent contains a list of things, the child has access to the individual list items. A common scenario for groups is to display something, like the current user, and then have the group contain the inputs that modify the user's properties, such as text and images that display information about the user. The Type of content determines what kind of thing you are allowed to set in the Data source field. If the type of thing does not match the Data source, it will be marked as an issue, and you must change one or the other to match. The Data source can come from anywhere in Bubble. For example, it could be the result of a search, a custom or built-in Data source, or it could be dynamic and come from the user. The Data source will update when its source changes. For example, if the search result changes because a user modified some data used in the search constraints, the displayed list will automatically change. For additional flexibility, you can update the Data source in the Conditional Formatting Tab in the Property Editor. Finally, the Data source property can be modified by workflows. Changes made by actions override whatever you specify as the Data source directly or as a conditional state until you run a reset action, which then restores the original setting.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#this-popup-cant-be-closed-by-pressing-esc)

This popup can't be closed by pressing 'Esc'

By default, popups can be closed by the user when pressing the _Esc_ button or clicking outside of the popup with the mouse. To disable this behavior and make the popup modal, check this box.

In this case, the only way to close the popup is with a Hide Popup action in a workflow.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#grayout-color)

Grayout color

Select the color of the background when a popup is open. By default, it is set to a dark transparent layer.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#grayout-blur)

Grayout blur

Enter a number greater than 0 to set a blur effect on the background. While this offers an interesting design, the display speed of the popup may be affected.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#start-edit-workflow-2)

Start/Edit workflow

Click this button to create a new workflow triggered by this element. The type of event depends on the element and is generally the most common event. For example, buttons will create a 'When this element is clicked...' event. If a workflow already exists, this takes you to the existing workflow in the Workflow Tab. To create a different type of event or create a second event, go to the Workflow Tab and click the 'Click here to add an event...' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#this-element-isnt-clickable-2)

This element isn't clickable

When checked, the element will not trigger a workflow when clicked nor will the user's cursor change to a pointer when they hover over the element. In addition, the item will display according to any specified conditional formatting for the 'isn't clickable' state. This can be used to stop a user from moving forward in a flow until some condition is true. For example, we might disable a specific action until the user is logged in. 'This input should not be empty' can also be used to prevent the user from clicking.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#floating-group)

Floating Group


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Watch our Academy quick tip on how to set up a floating group

Floating groups are groups that float on the screen on top of the page but are not contained within the page. These are useful to keep an element in the same place on the screen regardless of the scrolling position of the page or the visibility of groups within the page. Floating groups are useful for headers, buttons like 'Scroll to top,' etc.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#type-of-content-4)

Type of content

This allows you to select the type of thing this container displays. Most elements that have this property also have a Data source property, which lets you select the displayed thing or list of things. The value of the data source must match the selected type of content. Let's look at an example of a map with hotels marked. When you click a marker, a popup displays with the name of the hotel. The Type of content would be 'Hotel' and the Data source would be 'Map's current marker.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#data-source-4)

Data source

Define the thing or list of things that should be displayed in this container. For example, in groups or popups, only a single thing will display, but for repeating groups a list of things will display. Child elements can access the Data source from their parent. If the parent contains a list of things, the child has access to the individual list items. A common scenario for groups is to display something, like the current user, and then have the group contain the inputs that modify the user's properties, such as text and images that display information about the user. The Type of content determines what kind of thing you are allowed to set in the Data source field. If the type of thing does not match the Data source, it will be marked as an issue, and you must change one or the other to match. The Data source can come from anywhere in Bubble. For example, it could be the result of a search, a custom or built-in Data source, or it could be dynamic and come from the user. The Data source will update when its source changes. For examle, if the search result changes because a user modified some data used in the search constraints, the displayed list will automatically change. For additional flexibility, you can update the Data source in the Conditional Formatting Tab in the Property Editor. Finally, the Data source property can be modified by workflows. Changes made by actions override whatever you specify as the Data source directly or as a conditional state until you run a reset action, which then restores the original setting.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#vertically-float-relative-to)

Vertically float relative to

Using this dropdown menu, choose the base to calculate the position of the floating group. Choose from: – Top: The distance between the top edge of the group and the top of the user's screen remains constant, no matter the scrolling position of the page. This is the default. – Bottom: The distance between the bottom of the group and the user's screen remains constant no matter the scrolling position of the page. – Both: Both the top and bottom distances will remain constant, and a scrollbar will appear if the group is too short to show all its content. – Nothing: The floating group will not float, meaning that it behaves like a regular group. For example, the distance is calculated as the height between the top edge of the floating group and the top of the Bubble page. Then, it displays the floating group that distance from the top of the user's screen. Note: By placing the floating group high on the page and making it float relative to the bottom, the element may be 'above' the top of the screen and not display.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#horizontally-float-relative-to)

Horizontally float relative to

Use this dropdown menu to determine whether the element floats relative to the Right or Left. Right and Left refer to the horizontal edges of the page. This option is less common than vertical reference, but it is useful when you want an element to display no matter the page width.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#floating-zindex)

Floating zindex

Floating groups can be above other elements or beneath the page, and then be used as a background design option. Note that when a floating element is beneath elements, it will only visible if the page background is transparent

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#parallax-effect-factor)

Parallax effect factor

When a floating group is beneath the page, you can apply a parallax effect to give some depth to the page. A factor of 1 will remove any parallax effect, while a factor of 0 will keep the element fixed, and a factor of 2 will make the element move twice faster than the scrolling position. A negative number will have the element move into the different direction of the scroll movement.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#start-edit-workflow-3)

Start/Edit workflow

Click this button to create a new workflow triggered by this element. The type of event depends on the element and is generally the most common event. For example, buttons will create a 'When this element is clicked...' event. If a workflow already exists, this takes you to the existing workflow in the Workflow Tab. To create a different type of event or create a second event, go to the Workflow Tab and click the 'Click here to add an event...' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#this-element-isnt-clickable-3)

This element isn't clickable

When checked, the element will not trigger a workflow when clicked nor will the user's cursor change to a pointer when they hover over the element. In addition, the item will display according to any specified conditional formatting for the 'isn't clickable' state. This can be used to stop a user from moving forward in a flow until some condition is true. For example, we might disable a specific action until the user is logged in. 'This input should not be empty' can also be used to prevent the user from clicking.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#group-focus)

Group Focus


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Watch our Academy quick tip on how to set up a Group Focus

A group focus is a group that is automatically hidden when the user clicks outside of it. Position it relative to another element. This is especially useful for dropdown menus.

**Note:** Although the Group Focus container is fixed relative to the page, the iOS keyboard appear event can cause the Group Focus container to move to an unexpected location due to how Apple handles this event.

**Tip:** The same element can be used to open and close a group focus element. Best practice would be to use a "Toggle an element" workflow action on the element to simply open & close the group focus.

**Note:** If you have a searchbox within your Group Focus, clicking one of the searchbox's results will be considered clicking "outside" the Group Focus and therefore close it. A dropdown or custom searchbox are often used in these designs instead.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#reference-element)

Reference element

Specify an element to be the location reference for the group.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#offset-top)

Offset top

Pushes the group this number of pixels below the reference element.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#offset-left)

Offset left

Pushes the group this number of pixels left of the reference element.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#type-of-content-5)

Type of content

This allows you to select the type of thing this container displays. Most elements that have this property also have a Data source property, which lets you select the displayed thing or list of things. The value of the data source must match the selected type of content. Let's look at an example of a map with hotels marked. When you click a marker, a popup displays with the name of the hotel. The Type of content would be 'Hotel' and the Data source would be 'Map's current marker.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#data-source-5)

Data source

Define the thing or list of things that should be displayed in this container. For example, in groups or popups, only a single thing will display, but for repeating groups a list of things will display. Child elements can access the Data source from their parent. If the parent contains a list of things, the child has access to the individual list items. A common scenario for groups is to display something, like the current user, and then have the group contain the inputs that modify the user's properties, such as text and images that display information about the user. The Type of content determines what kind of thing you are allowed to set in the Data source field. If the type of thing does not match the Data source, it will be marked as an issue, and you must change one or the other to match. The Data source can come from anywhere in Bubble. For example, it could be the result of a search, a custom or built-in Data source, or it could be dynamic and come from the user. The Data source will update when its source changes. For examle, if the search result changes because a user modified some data used in the search constraints, the displayed list will automatically change. For additional flexibility, you can update the Data source in the Conditional Formatting Tab in the Property Editor. Finally, the Data source property can be modified by workflows. Changes made by actions override whatever you specify as the Data source directly or as a conditional state until you run a reset action, which then restores the original setting.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#collapse-this-elements-height-when-hidden)

Collapse this element's height when hidden

By default, hiding an element leaves a blank space on the page, exposing whatever is underneath it. When you select this option, however, the elements below the hidden element are pulled upward to fill the space.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#animate-the-collapse-operation-1)

Animate the collapse operation

This option is only visible if 'Collapse this element's height when hidden' is selected. By default, elements collapse and expand instantly, but this option animates with either Fade In/Out or Slide Up/Down.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#animation-style-1)

Animation style

This option allows you to choose whether an element's animation will Fade In/Out or Slide Up/Down.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#start-edit-workflow-4)

Start/Edit workflow

Click this button to create a new workflow triggered by this element. The type of event depends on the element and is generally the most common event. For example, buttons will create a 'When this element is clicked...' event. If a workflow already exists, this takes you to the existing workflow in the Workflow Tab. To create a different type of event or create a second event, go to the Workflow Tab and click the 'Click here to add an event...' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#this-element-isnt-clickable-4)

This element isn't clickable

When checked, the element will not trigger a workflow when clicked nor will the user's cursor change to a pointer when they hover over the element. In addition, the item will display according to any specified conditional formatting for the 'isn't clickable' state. This can be used to stop a user from moving forward in a flow until some condition is true. For example, we might disable a specific action until the user is logged in. 'This input should not be empty' can also be used to prevent the user from clicking.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#other-ways-to-learn)

Other ways to learn


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User manual articles[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#user-manual-articles)

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#containers-1)

Containers

This article series covers the different container element types in Bubble:

Article series: [Containers](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements/web-app/containers)

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#elements)

**Elements**

This article series covers how elements work, how to place them on the page to design your app and edit their attributes to make them look and behave like you want

Article series: [Elements](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements)

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#responsive-design)

Responsive design

Responsive design means to design your app so that it works on all screen sizes and resolutions (desktop, tablets and mobile) and make your elements behave in a consistent way with pixel-perfect design. Containers are an important part of responsive design.

Article series: [Responsive design](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/responsive-design)

Last updated 1 month ago

Was this helpful?

# Google | Bubble Docs

This is the short-form, technical reference for the Google plugin. For a more in-depth explanation of the plugin and how it works, see our User Manual article below:

Article: [The Google plugin](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/data/user-accounts/authentication-plugins/google-plugin)

This reference entry describes the main Google plugin, used to authenticate (create an account and logging in) a user in your app. For other specific Google features, we have dedicated reference entries as listed below:

Reference: [Google Analytics](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/google-analytics)
 Reference [Google Optimize](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/google-optimize)
 Reference: [Google Places](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/google-places)

The Google plugin lets your app's end-users authenticate with Google to sign up and log in to your app.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/google#settings)

Settings


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The plugin settings allow you to specify your App ID and App Secret token.

*   **App ID:** this string identifies your application with Google.
    
*   **Secret key:** this key is used to authenticate your app's access to Google. This key should be kept strictly confidential.
    

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/google#development-tokens)

Development tokens

You can also add development tokens as provided by Google, to test the API without it affecting live data. The information added here will automatically be used when you preview your app in the Development environment.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/google#actions)

Actions


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The plugin adds a new action to the Bubble editor that lets your end-users sign in and log in to your app using their Google account. These two processes are contained within one action:

`Account - Signup/login with a social network`

Bubble will automatically create a new account or log in an existing account, depending on whether the end-user already has a Google-authenticated account set up with your app.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/google#data-sources)

Data sources


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Google plugin adds five new data sources to your app. Note that all of these data sources are connected to the _Current User_. This means that they are accessed by using the _Current User_ data source, and then appending the source you wish to reach, such as:

`Current User's Google's First name`

The following data sources are added to the User data type:

*   ID: the unique ID of the user in Google's system
    
*   Email: the user's email address registered with Google The email will also automatically be saved in the _Current user's email_ field
    
*   First name
    
*   Last name
    
*   Profile picture
    

Last updated 1 year ago

Was this helpful?

---

# Fitbit | Bubble Docs

Let users log in with their Fitbit accounts, fetch their workout data, and update their subscription status.

This is the short-form, technical reference for the Fitbit plugin. For a more in-depth explanation of the plugin and how it works, see our User Manual article below:

Article: [The Fitbit plugin](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/data/user-accounts/authentication-plugins/fitbit-plugin)

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#signup-login-with-a-social-network)

**Signup/login with a social network**


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This workflow action allows you to integrate the Fitbit authentication event when registering a new user, or logging in an existing user.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#oauth-provider)

**OAuth provider**

Allows the Fitbit editor to verify the correct social platform to login/signup the user.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#invite-fitbit-friend)

**Invite Fitbit friend**


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Send a friend request to another Fitbit user.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#inviteduseremail)

InvitedUserEmail

The email address of a user’s Fitbit account that the current user would like to send an invite to.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#accept-fitbit-friend-invitation)

**Accept Fitbit friend invitation**


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Accept a friend request from another Fitbit user.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#fromuserid)

**FromUserID**

When choosing to accept a friend request, this is the ID of a Fitbit user's account that will be added to the current user’s friend list.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#add-fitbit-subscription)

**Add Fitbit subscription**


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Start a Fitbit subscription.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#subscriptionid)

**SubscriptionID**

The unique characters to reference a user's Fitbit subscription.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#delete-fitbit-subscription)

**Delete Fitbit subscription**


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Remove a Fitbit subscription.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#collectionpath)

**CollectionPath**

Collection of data to retrieve notifications. If the collection is not provided, the list will include all subscriptions for the user.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#current-users-fitbit-profile)

**Current user’s Fitbit profile**


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When adding dynamic data, this expression allows you to fetch and display the profile information from a user’s connected Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#id)

**ID**

The unique ID of a user’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#avatar)

**Avatar**

The profile photo from a user’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#fullname)

**FullName**

The full name from a user’s connected Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#nickname)

**Nickname**

The alternative name added to a user’s connected Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#aboutme)

**AboutMe**

The profile description from a user’s connected Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#age)

**Age**

The age of a user saved within their Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#height)

**Height**

The height of a user saved within their Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#weight)

**Weight**

The weight of a user saved within their Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#dateofbirth)

**DateOfBirth**

The birth date of a user saved within their Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#membersince)

**MemberSince**

The date that a user initially created their Fitbit account.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#current-users-fitbit-daily-activities)

**Current user’s Fitbit daily activities**


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Allows you to fetch the user’s workout data from a specific date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#activities)

Activities

A list of all the recorded activities from a user’s account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#goals_caloriesout)

Goals\_caloriesOut

Calorie burn goal represents either dynamic daily target from the premium trainer plan or manual calorie burn goal.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#goals_distance)

Goals\_distance

The target distance traveled within a user’s Fitbit account for a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#goals_floors)

Goals\_floors

The target amount of floors climbed within a user’s Fitbit account for a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#goals_steps)

Goals\_steps

The target number of steps within a user’s Fitbit account for a specified date.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#current-users-fitbit-food-log)

**Current user’s Fitbit food log**


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Allows you to fetch the user’s log food intake data from a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#foods)

Foods

The recorded foods a user consumed on a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#summary_calories)

Summary\_calories

A summary of the total calories consumed by a user on a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#summary_carbs)

Summary\_carbs

A summary of the total carbs consumed by a user on a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#summary_fat)

Summary\_fat

A summary of the total fat consumed by a user on a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#summary_fiber)

Summary\_fiber

A summary of the total fiber consumed by a user on a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#summary_protein)

Summary\_protein

A summary of the total protein consumed by a user on a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#summary_sodium)

Summary\_sodium

A summary of the total sodium consumed by a user on a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#summary_water)

Summary\_water

A summary of the total water consumed by a user on a specified date.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#current-users-fitbit-sleep-log)

Current user’s Fitbit sleep log


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Allows you to fetch the user’s logged sleep data from a specific date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#totalminutesasleep)

TotalMinutesAsleep

A summary of the total minutes a user slept on a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#totalsleeprecords)

TotalSleepRecords

The total number of sleep records stored within a user’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#totaltimeinbed)

TotalTimeInBed

A summary of the total minutes a user spent in bed - both awake and asleep.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#current-users-fitbit-heart-rate-log)

Current user’s Fitbit heart rate log


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Allows you to fetch the user’s heart rate data logged from a specific date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#caloriesout)

CaloriesOut

The total number of calories a user burnt on a specified date

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#max)

Max

The max heart rate recorded on a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#min)

Min

The minimum heart rate recorded on a specified date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#minutes)

Minutes

The duration of each session where a heart rate was elevated.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#name)

Name

The name given to a workout session where the user’s heart rate was elevated.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#current-users-fitbit-lifetime-activities)

Current user’s Fitbit lifetime activities


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The total activity statistics from a user’s Fitbit account. This is an overview of all their historical key data points, as well as achievement values.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#lifetime_total_caloriesout)

Lifetime\_total\_caloriesOut

The total number of calories a user has burnt since the creation of their account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#lifetime_total_distance)

Lifetime\_total\_distance

The total distance traveled by a user since the creation of their account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#lifetime_total_floors)

Lifetime\_total\_floors

The total number of floors a user has climbed since the creation of their account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#lifetime_total_activescore)

Lifetime\_total\_activeScore

The average active score given to a user’s account based on their lifetime activity.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#current-users-fitbit-device)

Current user’s Fitbit device


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Fitbit device model linked to the user’s account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#id-1)

ID

The device ID on a user’s linked Fitbit device.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#battery)

Battery

The current battery level of a user’s linked Fitbit device.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#deviceversion)

DeviceVersion

The device model of a user’s linked Fitbit device.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#type)

Type

The type of device linked to a user’s account e.g. fitness tracker, scales.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#lastsynctime)

LastSyncTime

The last recorded time that a user’s device was synced to their account.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#current-users-fitbit-friends)

Current user’s Fitbit friends


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A list of all the friends connected to a user’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#id-2)

ID

The unique ID of a friend’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#avatar-1)

Avatar

The profile photo of a friend’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#fullname-1)

FullName

The full name listed on a friend’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#nickname-1)

Nickname

The alternative name added to a friend’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#aboutme-1)

AboutMe

The profile description from a friend’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#age-1)

Age

The age saved on a friend’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#height-1)

Height

The height saved on a friend’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#weight-1)

Weight

The weight saved on a friend’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#dateofbirth-1)

DateOfBirth

The date of birth saved on a friend’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#membersince-1)

MemberSince

The date that a user’s friend registered initially created their Fitbit account.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#current-users-fitbit-friends-invitations)

Current user’s Fitbit friends invitations


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A list of the current friend requests to a user’s Fitbit account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#datetime)

DateTime

The date and time that a friend request was sent.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#user)

User

The user who has sent the current user a Fitbit friend invite.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#current-users-fitbit-subscription)

Current user’s Fitbit subscription


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Allows you to fetch the user’s heart rate data logged from a specific date.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#collectiontype)

CollectionType

The type of Fitbit subscription e.g. activities, body, sleep, etc

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#ownerid)

OwnerID

The ID of the user who owns the subscription.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#ownertype)

OwnerType

The type of entity who owns the current subscription e.g. user

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#subscriberid)

SubscriberID

The ID of the subscriber that will receive the subscription notifications.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#subscriptionid-1)

SubscriptionID

This is the unique ID of the subscription created by the API client application.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#setup)

**Setup**


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#configuring-your-api)

Configuring your API

Once you’ve installed the Fitbit plugin, you’ll need to first create a [developer account](https://dev.fitbit.com/getting-started/)
 to secure access to an OAuth key.

After registering a developer account, you’ll then need to register a new app within the Fitbit [developer portal](https://dev.fitbit.com/apps)
.

Once the details of your app have been added, you’ll be provided with a client ID and secret key for your OAuth 2.0 integration.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fkk4ASEA8Ugi7YhCLrppRmrYijvGdS5gsv6lAP0BjBBT4maZS1Yb-4Uaw9GC3SHPChyhSuSKr6ROUW70z_joiEYHsAnHyJxkTtcvqmJwsv3ni4MWkQ3ASx1cOW_hjerILjH_1AQAK%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=b9a9f411&sv=2)

Now, you’ll need to copy both the client ID and client secret keys into their relevant fields within your plugin settings.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh4.googleusercontent.com%2FZgAEB-uDmlS8zByD1EAhQwFnxBUxLfxhvQp7EaUWU5lLNXqBVgm997AAhI674BV5zs0FJ2iktj76IqY3EmASKOdLQbHmdn6BfMxP2vbn1q0Q6j6CopADmNwQGdaXGrqvX0ffWn5g%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=d005f511&sv=2)

Note: It’s also possible to enable a generic redirect URL within the Bubble plugin settings itself.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh6.googleusercontent.com%2FXUowXG7lO9t_nQu1wjKbv3hUi0XG9kcqsNmpOP0Yw5Im1Ta2746mv0BjlHXXN6h8FbAPYCcgWEE_eKItHEs9FF-PFH87d-ZB2FJxf_y35dv1LSzaPYPRjVML5wSGyPGU1SDT7dav%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=5d812d02&sv=2)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#logging-a-user-in-through-fitbit)

Logging a user in through Fitbit

After configuring your OAuth credentials, you can now use the ‘signup/login with social network’ step within a workflow. From the event dropdown menu, you’ll see an option to select the Fitbit OAuth provider. This event can be added to an element like a login button.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh6.googleusercontent.com%2FX1dpv1mF8RCU1D9KgLNjeSMa3EvoBXVK37hH-dOyAMqbqiPGjb7iwS_VkAXdQtpxgtbHhoxkqwJx-tzEZ1d7-aJcllURsSiosk-sZmL0M9eyASMJMTYI6veD4ioTGFPQMjtlOnOH%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=2e40f8e7&sv=2)

After a user clicks this button, they’ll be redirected to a Fitbit portal where they can verify their account, then be redirected back to the destination URL of your application.

Now, you can choose to display information from a user's Fitbit account by using the ‘current user’s Fitbit’ operator.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh3.googleusercontent.com%2Fm_F9w1nN6a6_pVlQDujMJnFk5Pld7mgcSLXHMKE8fNpZuzrcr5W64xo_lb0BnkzroaF8zncvMxPU4E82_sxP3L4DqjEwRGTikSHVYCm__uHjlgvH17PpneLDtx39JFXundPmakyy%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=c2609f15&sv=2)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#accepting-a-friend-invitation)

Accepting a friend invitation

To display a list of a user’s Fitbit friend invitations, start by adding a repeating group element to the page. The type of content for this repeating group will need to be configured as ‘Fitbit Friend Invitation’.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh3.googleusercontent.com%2FyJdtuJGkE2s1-fjYD69yrPPMyjNtuAbiLld3nRFVplu1zA6gbc064jFhHy6KcQBb72Uq_U_WhZarXpxaVzxycbNAL2hiUpHTfoyneomMq-ZdE_ew_UYiDOI4Ruip-F5vrwfNmQdd%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=a143b84b&sv=2)

Now, you’ll also need to update the data source to display the ‘current user’s Fitbit friends invitations’.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh6.googleusercontent.com%2Fi98_jnpzr5M2y24l2KBjS72pNOwnj84jba2QvANEgXZWB9XcPJDOKiznHM6m3pzh3bc2pU2K7XVoaf6f71KPHpQRzOTCwtLa7oUATnE-uc1HBq-jxCPDNhAXL8JmV4m4vsv0AidD%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=352ac8f7&sv=2)

Now, within the repeating group cell, you can display information from a user’s Fitbit profile who has sent a friend invitation.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh3.googleusercontent.com%2F49sbun1F3dl76F5mfAk9K3ksgM2z5TWVHykWPgQuPoFiXWh3N-fszACbZhUYrehyZp_FH11VEdUdiXgcThJfeGhLAocFTEdcyeFVPnxxO5wnpKbrcgaFTkNiq6WMHMFv1aTquKWL%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=8b4965e3&sv=2)

From here, when the accept button is clicked, you can create a workflow and choose from the ‘accept Fitbit friend invitation’ event.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fq4WA32gpcqLH-Q6bSr7w44Tvv2frhOVUKHCYcEiZbCQiDUNY3rItm1cP1PLLiyhfvGs_7B09JBuaZW5k8-S28Fe8RMCZ5m9ra9XRsdde2mIbpd0-0VyXyq8qRMfWmdxHmBHsEqPw%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=9e103afa&sv=2)

When configuring this workflow event, you’ll need to set the FromUserID to be the ‘current cell’s Fitbit friend’s invitation’s User ID’.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh4.googleusercontent.com%2FED36EjgSx2PI-lu-AD_Wa-YigLCVb31z4bGOPMs7rL-gPJssUD4Qxj4rfYVBE21_9FHVXm32CuMyTaFQ6DlnQEYNClvzG804_nDlDRdEwTvdko4L-NTq84kJRVph72ytZ_aE-AvL%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=b5e2528d&sv=2)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#inviting-a-fitbit-friend)

Inviting a Fitbit friend

In order to allow the current user to send a Fitbit friend invitation, select the ‘invite Fitbit friend’ workflow event.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh3.googleusercontent.com%2FV8l1xwtkaWo2nABLS7N6P9Mkj0hlMJvemNPGrPFalXK-DUtrkuzOXGHCfGiN0S0uWz0bk8Fk7z7z_mg2pJjsFn9RpkwWMjFJLU2mrheSaA07EBGV6wJnydDxzdGlYGA1ebNwWR56%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=a4f59dab&sv=2)

Within this workflow event, you’ll need to configure an email value associated with a user’s Fitbit account. This can be attained through an input form.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh6.googleusercontent.com%2FIzOghwqVC9XL8_5h0Sm2EbXzapX_EFSOzDm7BTZuRAaTkC1M8L8mpqw_RZjz5AcdeyCieJPVNnu7gENcFvR74KpjbMs4D7wig5hRUT7d3dgXy6ZVOUbWCMSrcCM8-ljXCJZVqseO%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=30e04027&sv=2)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#adding-a-fitbit-subscription)

Adding a Fitbit subscription

When adding a new Fitbit subscription, select the ‘add a Fitbit subscription’ event within your workflow editor.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh3.googleusercontent.com%2FdAC6Y5PItWcL_aEIOONqFzOuSTDZ3rdPy7eMYH6U-9xV2e7oRIufRpsG98vwIaqqzwDFiNgMnZZtrOCfoN3auuTHKqSkHF0xUu4Vy6Y-EFKFV84wOkK42numSaXrVu3RLNPPkzec%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=e272050e&sv=2)

When adding a new subscriber, you’ll need to associate a SubscriptionID to the current user. This unique ID will be shared with Fitbit. As Fitbit states within their [API docs](https://dev.fitbit.com/build/reference/web-api/subscriptions/)
, this ID has a character limit of 50 characters.

Within a Bubble input, it’s possible to use the formula operator to generate a random character string.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh4.googleusercontent.com%2FOF0XDRL5nDirbCqPDQqdQ186osENWgTtbiuKXngM93P-V8G3lySGyyeTXm0s-QXKruPV0M5FJrRAe7qSxKobRrWzbzZmCFT-kcP8khAUYXYyXe_jQoeO4MpeXXcbapi97QQzigpL%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=52f53f73&sv=2)

Now, when configuring your subscription workflow event, it’s possible to use the value of this input field as the SubscriptionID.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh4.googleusercontent.com%2Fd3Kgm7QLX8KbpRcLpy8WIuphZxoWUKhkpnzc1qU43clHwe5crSEJCoIxCcZ5hOmADKETCF6Ni9gugeyLcK_1zUiGwZPF4GVWebRhUPIAQJwakjApOS7JMZqROPwDn7NuBb3SjtDW%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=308bc5ee&sv=2)

We’d also recommend storing this SubscriptionID as a field under your user data type. This will allow you to reference this unique ID if the user chooses to delete their subscription at a later date.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh4.googleusercontent.com%2Fly-S5HDCo_SxFRW55iomNSGjQPwxAoocmRvP8hIpUjhGyhMegtnIxsTZwUG4pqsoCE0M3PTuJBjjaGQJYuZXPpn_jC4dpW0w4jo4Nf6HjHXerZ4ZH0uVijtNnzuo8yFBS4qTByBH%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=c673b549&sv=2)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#deleting-a-fitbit-subscription)

Deleting a Fitbit subscription

When deleting a user’s Fitbit subscription, select from the ‘delete a Fitbit subscription’ workflow event.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh6.googleusercontent.com%2FbAu791I5VldUHXN_Lvgfwu66afGSboFScuxFmp7YlYP8Nzlqfw9jm5JJmNtnafVtN8M1JW-eEOBJMa6xcKalo65gTeS6BDzbRvkXcG5B__1XGPQWWLfKCWtjxWqdP1K2Ixhuev7n%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=77e61396&sv=2)

When configuring this workflow step, you’ll need to provide the subscription type that the user will be canceling.

Note: If the CollectionPath is not provided, subscriptions will be deleted from all collections.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh6.googleusercontent.com%2FU27XNWeWivGzNzPBArozQSiMMkEe19XGyDDDxGnN4fsYzNsNvThjIsnhm6mQbh8uQNvLueqXvHcPr8KF75zpI4rtEjWBG6TGmG-1NGBWCK1AO8SHXsrxnaV5B6-icmAylJ9e5FQJ%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=5fe23830&sv=2)

Next, you’ll need to verify what subscription ID you’ll be deleting. If you’ve stored the current user’s subscription ID within your database, you can reference this here.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh6.googleusercontent.com%2F6iTARb8n1uitbtCuf6gPSgfgcp9iqzOG3cqHfHK8n3BsXxf44bY1Se9niUZFBkYxPgYULjCeyszKyO96bTjXBzQELvF4j39-Of12MpSaPt6WGhHWCdXU0qI_YZuhPkVujX6zZfLU%3Ds0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=3fa90723&sv=2)

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/fitbit#faq)

**FAQ**


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

(Have a suggested FAQ to help other Bubblers? Please visit our [Support center](https://bubble.io/support)
 to get in touch with a member of our Support team!)

Last updated 1 year ago

Was this helpful?

---

# Stripe | Bubble Docs

The Stripe Plugin charges credit cards and handles subscriptions for users. Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) is supported and can be enabled from the plugin menu by selecting “Checkout v3” in the dropdown.

See [Stripe’s documentation](https://stripe.com/docs)
 for more information.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#charge-the-current-user)

Charge the current user


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action charges the current user and prompts them to enter their credit card information. The response from Stripe, e.g., the Stripe charge ID, amount, confirmation, etc., can be accessed in the subsequent actions as 'Result of previous step.' In a failed transaction, the workflow will stop running. See [Stripe's documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/api#charge_object)
 for more details.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#payer-email)

Payer email

Define the email of the user to charge. Often, it will be 'Current user's email.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#amount)

Amount

Enter the amount to be charged. It can be dynamic using the 'Insert dynamic data' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#name)

Name

Enter the product name that will appear in the checkout flow and in the email users receive from Stripe.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#description)

Description

Enter the description that will appear in the checkout flow and in the email users receive from Stripe.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#image)

Image

The image that will appear in the checkout flow.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#currency)

Currency

Enter the currency of the transaction. If the currency is not in the list you can type it.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#button-caption)

Button caption

This is the caption for the button on the Stripe Checkout form. The amount will be added to the caption in Live mode.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#statement-descriptor)

Statement descriptor

Optional, use this to change how this payment will show up on the customer's statement. What you enter will be appended to the "shortened descriptor set in your Stripe account, in the manner of "YOUR-APP\*DESCRIPTOR". This overall string has a character limit of 21; anything past that will be truncated. In Stripe's v3 checkout, this field will comprise the entirety of the statement descriptor.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#authorize-the-charge-only)

Authorize the charge only

This determines whether to immediately capture the charge. When checked, the charge issues an authorization and will need to be captured later. Uncaptured charges expire in 7 days. You can then capture the charge in your Stripe dashboard.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#the-payee-of-this-transaction-is-another-user)

The payee of this transaction is another user

When setting up a marketplace, check this box so payment will go to another user instead of your own Stripe account. For this to work, you must build a flow where users can [register as a seller](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#register-the-user-as-a-seller)
, connecting their Stripe account to get paid.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#transaction-payee)

Transaction payee

In the case of a marketplace, define who gets the payment. This is likely the 'Current list thing's creator.' This user must register as a seller for the transaction to process.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#app-fee)

App fee

You can charge a fee on transactions, but it must a currency amount and not a percentage.You can, however, compute the value in the field using the Composer.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#allow-promotion-codes-v3)

Allow promotion codes (v3)

Check this box to allow your users to enter a promotion or discount code when they are sent to checkout. Coupon codes can be created in your Stripe Dashboard. See [Stripe Documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/billing/subscriptions/discounts/codes)
 for more details.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#add-tax-rate-to-checkout-v3)

Add tax rate to checkout (v3)

Check this box to add a tax rate or list of tax rates to the user's invoice. Before you check this box, be sure to create your [Tax Rate](https://stripe.com/docs/payments/checkout/taxes)
 objects in your Stripe Dashboard. If the transaction payee is another user, make sure the Tax Rate object is from the Connected account, not your own Stripe account.

While Stripe offers both Fixed & Dynamic Tax rates, the Stripe Plugin only supports Fixed tax rates at this time. See [Stripe Documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/payments/checkout/taxes)
 to learn more about Fixed vs Dynamic tax rates.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#tax-rate-ids-source)

Tax rate IDs source

This dropdown element allows you to input your tax rate IDs as static choices or dynamic data from the application database or Option set. In this dropdown menu, choose between Static IDs or Dynamic IDs.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#tax-rate-ids-static)

Tax rate IDs (press Enter between each ID)

This static input allows you to paste in your Stripe tax rate ID or list of tax rate IDs by separating each entry with a new line. All tax rates entered here will be applied to the user checkout session.

####
