[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#tax-rate-ids-dynamic)

Tax rate IDs

This dynamic input allows you to select a list of tax rate ids dynamically from your application, whether from the database, option set, or other datasource. This selection must evaluate to a list of texts.

If you'd like to specify a single tax rate ID, use the [:converted to list](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/data/operations-and-comparisons#converted-to-list)
 operator. See the example below:

If your tax rate IDs are stored in an [Option set](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/structuring-an-application/option-sets)
 named Tax Rates, and you'd like to select a single option named "Sales Tax" with the attribute "ID", your dynamic expression would look like:`Sales Tax:converted to list's ID`

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#do-not-show-success-message)

Do not show success message

By default, Bubble displays an alert informing the user when the action is successful. Deactivate this behavior by deselecting this box if you want to display a custom message instead of the default Bubble message.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#collect-the-users-cc-information)

Collect the user's CC information


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action prompts the user to enter their credit card information without actually charging the card. Use this to delay the payment, for instance, if you are building a crowdfunding-based system. If the user fails to enter their card information, the workflow will stop running.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#update-existing-main-card)

Update existing main card

If you check this box, the current user’s default credit card, if any, will be updated. However, the original credit card will still exist in the current user’s full list of credit cards unless it is explicitly deleted in a separate workflow action. If the box is not checked, the new credit card will be added to the current user’s full list of credit cards, but their default credit card will not change.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#do-not-show-success-message-1)

Do not show success message

By default, Bubble displays an alert informing the user when the action is successful. Deactivate this behavior by deselecting this box if you want to display a custom message instead of the default Bubble message.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#charge-a-user-using-saved-cc)

Charge a user using saved CC


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action charges a user who has already entered their credit card information. It does not have to be the current user in this case. If no credit card information is available, the action will fail. The response from Stripe, e.g., the Stripe charge ID, amount, confirmation, etc., can be accessed in the subsequent actions as 'Result of previous step.' In a failed transaction, the workflow will stop running.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#charged-user)

Charged user

Define the user to charge. This user must be signed up already because we need to have their saved credit card information. The user does not need to be logged in for the transaction to happen.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#amount-1)

Amount

Enter the amount to charge. It can be dynamic using the 'Insert dynamic data' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#currency-1)

Currency

Enter the currency of the transaction. If the currency is not in the list you can type it.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#name-1)

Name

Enter the product name that will appear in the checkout flow and in the email users receive from Stripe.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#description-1)

Description

Enter the description that will appear in the checkout flow and in the email users receive from Stripe.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#image-1)

Image

The image that will appear in the checkout flow.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#the-payee-of-this-transaction-is-another-user-1)

The payee of this transaction is another user

When setting up a marketplace, check this box so payment will go to another user instead of your own Stripe account. For this to work, you must build a flow where users can [register as a seller](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#register-the-user-as-a-seller)
, connecting their Stripe account to get paid.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#transaction-payee-1)

Transaction payee

In the case of a marketplace, define who gets the payment. This is likely the 'Current list thing's creator.' This user must register as a seller for the transaction to process.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#app-fee-1)

App fee

You can charge a fee on transactions, but it must a currency amount and not a percentage.You can, however, compute the value in the field using the Composer.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#card-id-to-charge)

Card ID to charge

You can optionally specific a card ID to charge. If this field is not filled, the default card of the user will be used.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#do-not-show-success-message-2)

Do not show success message

By default, Bubble displays an alert informing the user when the action is successful. Deactivate this behavior by deselecting this box if you want to display a custom message instead of the default Bubble message.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#capture-an-authorized-charge)

Capture an authorized charge


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action lets you capture a charge that has already been authorized by Stripe. Note that uncaptured charges expire after 7 days, per Stripe's documentation.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#charge-id)

Charge ID

Charge ID of the charge you want to capture.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#do-not-show-success-message-3)

Do not show success message

By default, Bubble displays an alert informing the user when the action is successful. Deactivate this behavior by deselecting this box if you want to display a custom message instead of the default Bubble message.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#create-an-invoice)

Create an invoice


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action creates a draft invoice for the user. It contains all pending invoice items on that user. The drafted invoice cannot be paid or sent until it is finalized.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#user)

User

Define the user to charge. If the user does not have a credit card on file, the action will fail.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-id)

Subscription ID

If no Subscription ID is given, all pending invoice items for the charged user will be included in the created invoice. Otherwise, the created invoice will include both pending invoice items for that subscription and pending invoice items not associated with any subscription. Billing cycle and regular subscription events associated to that subscription will not be affected.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#automatic-collection)

Automatic collection

When the invoice fails to be charged, you can enable this feature to allow Stripe's Smart Retries to automatically choose optimal times to retry failed payments. If set to false, no actions will be taken to advance the invoice's state automatically.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#update-an-invoice)

Update an invoice


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action updates the details of an invoice.

Only invoice drafts can be edited.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#invoice-id)

Invoice ID

The ID of the invoice you want to update.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#description-2)

Description

Extra string of text that can be added to the invoice. Visible to the recipient of the invoice. This field is referenced as "Memo" in the Stripe Dashboard.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#auto-advance)

Auto advance

Controls whether Stripe will perform automatic collection of the invoice.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#register-the-user-as-a-seller)

Register the user as a seller


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action enables users to become merchants on your platform. For example, if you want to build a marketplace where users can sell their work to someone else, then they need to be able to credit their account when a transaction occurs. Your app will usually take a commission, which can be set when setting up the actions "[Charge a user using saved cc](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#charge-a-user-using-saved-cc)
" and "[Charge the current user](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#charge-the-current-user)
". You must create an application on Stripe to enable this type of transaction.

In v3 of the plugin, the registering seller will be directed to a Stripe-hosted seller onboarding experience.

At this time, only Connect **Standard** account onboarding is supported with this method.

See [Stripe Connect](https://stripe.com/connect)
 for more information. _Important:_ You need to set the redirection URIs in Stripe as 'https://bubble.io/poststripeauth' or 'https://d##.bubble.is/poststripeauth' for Dedicated environments in order for this to work. In addition, be sure to add the Connect client ID to your Stripe Plugin settings. Both the client ID and redirect URI settings can be found in your Stripe Dashboard > Settings > Connect settings.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#business-email)

Business email

Prefill information to ease the process with Stripe. Enter the email the user is likely to use.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#business-url)

Business URL

Prefill information to ease the process with Stripe. Enter the URL the user is likely to use.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#business-name)

Business name

Prefill information to ease the process with Stripe. Enter the name the user is likely to use.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#business-address)

Business address

Prefill information to ease the process with Stripe. Enter the address the user is likely to use.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#product-category)

Product category

Prefill information to ease the process with Stripe. Enter the product category the user is likely to sell.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#sold-products-are-physical-products)

Sold products are physical products

Prefill information to ease the process with Stripe. Enter whether the user is likely to sell a physical product.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#product-description)

Product description

Prefill information to ease the process with Stripe. Enter the type of product the user is likely to sell.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscribe-the-user-to-a-plan)

Subscribe the user to a plan


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action subscribes a user to a plan, which was previously defined in Stripe. Then, the card will be charged on a regular basis. If the user is already on a plan, their subscription will be updated to the new plan. If the user fails to enter their credit card information, the workflow will stop running.

Since the app has two versions, Development and Live, you need to have both versions of your account in Stripe to have the same plans with the same IDs.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#apply-this-action-to-the-current-user)

Apply this action to the current user

By default, the 'Subscribe user' action applies to the currently logged in user. To apply the operation to another user, uncheck this box.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscribed-user)

Subscribed user

Define the user to subscribe to a plan. This user must be signed up already because we need to have their saved credit card information. The user does not need to be logged in for the transaction to happen.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#update-existing-subscription)

Update existing subscription

If you check this box, the current user's subscription, if any, will be updated. If the box is not check, a new subscription will be added to the user.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-id-opt)

Subscription ID (opt.)

If you want to update an existing subscription, you can define here the subscription ID (returned by Stripe). If this is empty, the action will modify the first subscription returned by Stripe.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#dynamically-specify-plan)

Dynamically specify plan

By default, the plan is chosen in a dropdown menu. To make the plan dynamic, check this box.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-type)

Subscription type

You can either pick one plan (most common case), or include more than one plan in the subscription.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#plan-name)

Plan name

Once you enter your keys in the Stripe section in the Plugins Tab, we fetch the plans you created with Stripe to populate this list. Select the plan to subscribe the user to. If the plan is dynamic, the value must be one of the plans defined in your Stripe account.

Since the app has two versions, Development and Live, you need to have both versions of your account in Stripe to have the same plans with the same IDs.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#quantity)

Quantity

Enter the quantity to apply to the plan. In most cases it will be 1, but to have variable pricing on the plan, use this.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-items)

Subscription Items

You can define a list of plans/quantity to add to the subscriptions

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#apply-a-coupon-to-this-subscription-v2)

Apply a coupon to this subscription (v2)

To provide discounts to certain customers, create coupon codes in the Stripe Dashboard. Check this box to enter a coupon ID.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#coupon-id)

Coupon ID

Enter the coupon ID. It can be dynamic information coming from an input.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#payment-behavior)

Payment behavior

Select the payment behavior as specified in the Stripe documentation. See [this link](https://stripe.com/docs/api/subscriptions/create#create_subscription-payment_behavior)
 for creating a new subscription and [this link](https://stripe.com/docs/api/subscriptions/update#update_subscription-payment_behavior)
 for updating an existing subscription.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#allow-promotion-codes-v3-1)

Allow promotion codes (v3)

Check this box to allow your users to enter a promotion or discount code when they are sent to checkout. Coupon codes can be created in your Stripe Dashboard. See [Stripe Documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/billing/subscriptions/discounts/codes)
 for more details.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-add-tax-rate-to-checkout-v-3)

Add tax rate to checkout (v3)

Check this box to add a tax rate or list of tax rates to the user's invoice. Before you check this box, be sure to create your [Tax Rate](https://stripe.com/docs/billing/taxes/tax-rates)
 objects in your Stripe Dashboard.

While Stripe offers both Fixed & Dynamic Tax rates, the Stripe Plugin only supports Fixed tax rates at this time. See [Stripe Documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/billing/taxes/tax-rates)
 to learn more about Fixed vs Dynamic tax rates.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-tax-rate-ids-source)

Tax rate IDs source

This dropdown element allows you to input your tax rate IDs as static choices or dynamic data from the application database or Option set. In this dropdown menu, choose between Static IDs or Dynamic IDs.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-tax-rate-ids-static)

Tax rate IDs (press Enter between each ID)

This static input allows you to paste in your Stripe tax rate ID or list of tax rate IDs by separating each entry with a new line. All tax rates entered here will be applied to the user checkout session.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-tax-rate-ids-dynamic)

Tax rate IDs

This dynamic input allows you to select a list of tax rate ids dynamically from your application, whether from the database, option set, or other datasource. This selection must evaluate to a list of texts.

If you'd like to specify a single tax rate ID, use the [:converted to list](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/data/operations-and-comparisons#converted-to-list)
 operator. See the example below:

If your tax rate IDs are stored in an [Option set](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/structuring-an-application/option-sets)
 named Tax Rates, and you'd like to select a single option named "Sales Tax" with the attribute "ID", your dynamic expression would look like:`Sales Tax:converted to list's ID`

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#tax-percent)

Tax percent (v2)

Enter the tax percentage, which should be a positive decimal between 1 and 100. It can be dynamic using the Composer. This represents the percentage of the subscription invoice subtotal that will be calculated and added as tax to the final amount for each billing period. For example, a plan that charges $10/month with a tax percent of 20.0 will charge $12 per invoice.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#end-of-the-trial)

End of the trial

Enter the date representing the end of the trial period. After this time, the customer will be charged. When selected, this setting overrides the default trial period of the plan the customer is subscribed to.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#do-not-show-success-message-4)

Do not show success message

By default, Bubble displays an alert informing the user when the action is successful. Deactivate this behavior by deselecting this box if you want to display a custom message instead of the default Bubble message.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#update-stripe-customer)

Update Stripe Customer


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action modifies email and address for an existing Stripe customer.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#apply-this-action-to-the-current-user-1)

Apply this action to the current user

By default, the 'Subscribe user' action applies to the currently logged in user. To apply the operation to another user, uncheck this box.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscribed-user-1)

Subscribed User

Define the user to subscribe to a plan. This user must be signed up already because we need to have their saved credit card information. The user does not need to be logged in for the transaction to happen.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#email)

Email

Enter the new email user would like to update to

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#address)

Address

Enter the new address user would like to update to

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#create-a-stripe-coupon)

Create a Stripe Coupon


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action creates a coupon which can be used by any Stripe customer.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#coupon-name)

Coupon Name

Name of the coupon displayed to customers

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#amount-off)

Amount Off

Amount (in the `currency` specified) that will be taken off via the coupon

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#percent-off)

Percent Off

Percentage that will be taken off via the coupon

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#duration)

Duration

How long a customer can apply the coupon for. Choose one from `forever`, `once`, and `repeating`.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#duration-in-months)

Duration in Months

If `duration` is `repeating`, the number of months the coupon applies.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#expiration-date)

**Expiration date**

After this date, the coupon will be no longer valid

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#currency-2)

**Currency**

Enter the currency of the coupon. If the currency is not in the list you can type it.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#max-redemption)

**Max redemption**

Maximum number of times a customer can redeem the coupon for

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#apply-a-coupon-to-the-users-subscription)

Apply a coupon to the user's subscription


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action applies a coupon/discount to the current user's subscription

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#apply-this-action-to-the-current-user-2)

Apply this action to the current user

By default, the 'Apply a coupon to the user's subscription' action applies to the currently logged in user. To apply the operation to another user, uncheck this box.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscribed-user-2)

Subscribed user

Define the user to apply a coupon to. This user must be signed up already because we need to have their saved credit card information. The user does not need to be logged in for the transaction to happen.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-id-1)

Subscription ID

If you want to update an existing subscription, you can define here the subscription ID (returned by Stripe). If this is empty, the action will modify the customer object in Stripe and all plans will have the coupon applied to them.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#end-of-the-trial-1)

End of the trial

Enter the date when the trial period should end. If left empty, no trial period will apply.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#do-not-show-success-message-5)

Do not show success message

By default, Bubble displays an alert informing the user when the action is successful. Deactivate this behavior by deselecting this box if you want to display a custom message instead of the default Bubble message.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#create-an-invoice-item)

Create an invoice item


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action creates an invoice item for the user. The invoice item will be paid with the next invoice.

**Note:** Since the introduction of the Bubble Stripe plugin, Stripe has clarified and updated some of its terminology. For this plugin, what Bubble calls a "line item" is what Stripe calls an "invoice line item" - these have Stripe IDs starting with 'il\_' and are entries on an existing invoice. The change Stripe made was to have its own concept of "line items" which correspond to future entries on the next invoice; these Stripe IDs start with 'ii\_' and can exist separately from an invoice. Bubble still uses its original definition of 'line items' (i.e. as Stripe's 'invoice line items') for the time being.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#user-1)

User

Define the user to charge. If the user does not have a credit card on file, the action will fail.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#amount-2)

Amount

Enter the amount to charge. It can be dynamic using the 'Insert dynamic data' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#currency-3)

Currency

Enter the currency of the transaction. If the currency is not in the list you can type it.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#description-3)

Description

Enter the description that will appear in the email users receive from Stripe.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-id-2)

Subscription ID

To add the invoice item to an existing subscription, specify the Stripe Subscription ID. This is an optional field.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#add-tax-rate-to-invoice-item)

Add tax rate to invoice item

Check this box to add a tax rate or list of tax rates to the user's invoice item. Before you check this box, be sure to create your [Tax Rate](https://stripe.com/docs/billing/taxes/tax-rates)
 objects in your Stripe Dashboard.

While Stripe offers both Fixed & Dynamic Tax rates, the Stripe Plugin only supports Fixed tax rates at this time. See [Stripe Documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/billing/taxes/tax-rates)
 to learn more about Fixed vs Dynamic tax rates.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-tax-rate-ids-source-1)

Tax rate IDs source

This dropdown element allows you to input your tax rate IDs as static choices or dynamic data from the application database or Option set. In this dropdown menu, choose between Static IDs or Dynamic IDs.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-tax-rate-ids-static-1)

Tax rate IDs (press Enter between each ID)

This static input allows you to paste in your Stripe tax rate ID or list of tax rate IDs by separating each entry with a new line. All tax rates entered here will be applied to the user checkout session.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-tax-rate-ids-dynamic-1)

Tax rate IDs

This dynamic input allows you to select a list of tax rate ids dynamically from your application, whether from the database, option set, or other datasource. This selection must evaluate to a list of texts.

If you'd like to specify a single tax rate ID, use the [:converted to list](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/data/operations-and-comparisons#converted-to-list)
 operator. See the example below:

If your tax rate IDs are stored in an [Option set](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/structuring-an-application/option-sets)
 named Tax Rates, and you'd like to select a single option named "Sales Tax" with the attribute "ID", yo

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#pay-an-invoice)

Pay an invoice


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action finalizes a draft invoice and immediately charges a pending invoice. It uses the card the user has on file.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#invoice-id-1)

Invoice ID

Enter the Stripe invoice ID to charge.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#cancel-an-invoice)

Cancel an Invoice


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action cancels an invoice by setting its status to void or uncollectible. For more information, check out the Stripe documentation for [Void an Invoice](https://stripe.com/docs/api/invoices/void)
 and [Mark as Uncollectible](https://stripe.com/docs/api/invoices/mark_uncollectible)
.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#invoice-id-2)

Invoice ID

Enter the Stripe invoice ID to cancel.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#status)

Status

Select the invoice's new status (void or uncollectible)

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#create-a-refund)

Create a refund


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action fully refunds a previously created charge that has not already been refunded.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#charge-id-1)

Charge ID

Charge ID of the charge you want to refund.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#cancel-the-current-users-plan)

Cancel the current user's plan


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action cancels a subscription with Stripe. The card will not be charged anymore.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#apply-this-action-to-the-current-user-3)

Apply this action to the current user

By default, the 'Cancel Plan' action applies to the currently logged in user. To apply the operation to another user, uncheck this box.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscribed-user-3)

Subscribed user

Define the user to unsubscribe. This user must be signed up already. The user does not need to be logged in for the transaction to happen.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#cancel-all-subscriptions)

Cancel all subscriptions

If this box is checked, all subscriptions on the user will be canceled. If not, you will be able to specify a subscription ID.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-id-3)

Subscription ID

If you want to cancel an existing subscription (and keep the other subscriptions running), you can define here the subscription ID (returned by Stripe).

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#do-not-show-success-message-6)

Do not show success message

By default, Bubble displays an alert informing the user when the action is successful. Deactivate this behavior by deselecting this box if you want to display a custom message instead of the default Bubble message.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#cancelation-mode)

Cancelation mode

By default, plans will be canceled immediately when using this action (set to _Immediately_). If you would prefer to wait until the end of the subscription’s billing period to cancel the user's plan, you can set the cancelation mode to _End of billing period._

This will update the subscription interface in Stripe with the upcoming cancelation date, shown as 'Cancels \[Date\]', where '\[Date\]' is the end of the plan's billing period:

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F34394582-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F-M5sbzwG7CljeZdkntrL%252Fuploads%252F7fs7uTFtwOQUaDBufAkg%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D7b5a6e1d-c95a-4ebd-83f1-7be7fcc11012&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=163ead67&sv=2)

The setting _End of billing period_ is an **advanced setting**. It is important that you have set up a Stripe webhook so that you can be notified by Stripe of changes and respond to the event ‘customer.subscription.deleted’. This event is fired at the end of the billing period when Stripe ends the subscription, however, your web app will not update unless you process the webhook event.

Please see our guide to [webhooks](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/api/introduction-to-apis#webhooks)
 in Bubble and Stripe’s guide to [webhooks](https://docs.stripe.com/webhooks)
 for more information.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#create-a-subscription-item)

Create a subscription item


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action adds a plan to an existing subscription.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-id-4)

Subscription ID

Define here the subscription ID (returned by Stripe) to add the plan to.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#plan-name-1)

Plan name

Once you enter your keys in the Stripe section in the Plugins Tab, we fetch the plans you created with Stripe to populate this list. Select the plan to subscribe the user to. If the plan is dynamic, the value must be one of the plans defined in your Stripe account.

Since the app has two versions, Development and Live, you need to have both versions of your account in Stripe to have the same plans with the same IDs.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#quantity-1)

Quantity

Enter the quantity to apply to the plan. In most cases it will be 1, but to have variable pricing on the plan, use this.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#update-a-subscription-item)

Update a subscription item


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action modifies a plan to an existing subscription that has more than one plan.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-item-id)

Subscription Item ID

Define here the subscription item D (returned by Stripe) to update.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#plan-name-2)

Plan name

Once you enter your keys in the Stripe section in the Plugins Tab, we fetch the plans you created with Stripe to populate this list. Select the plan to subscribe the user to. If the plan is dynamic, the value must be one of the plans defined in your Stripe account.

Since the app has two versions, Development and Live, you need to have both versions of your account in Stripe to have the same plans with the same IDs.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#quantity-2)

Quantity

Enter the quantity to apply to the plan. In most cases it will be 1, but to have variable pricing on the plan, use this.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#delete-a-subscription-item)

Delete a subscription item


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action deletes a plan to an existing subscription that has more than one plan. The subscription will still keep going after the deletion.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#subscription-item-id-1)

Subscription Item ID

Define here the subscription item ID (returned by Stripe) to delete.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#make-a-card-new-default)

Make a card new default


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action modifies the current user and makes a card the new default payment mean.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#credit-card-id)

Credit Card ID

Define here the card ID (returned by Stripe) to use.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#delete-a-credit-card)

Delete a credit card


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action deletes a credit card on the current user.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#credit-card-id-1)

Credit Card ID

Define here the card ID (returned by Stripe) to delete.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#retrieve-all-user-invoices)

Retrieve all user invoices


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This user call retrieves all user invoices based on the email of the user.

Because of limitations associated with the Stripe API, we can only retrieve up to 100 invoices per user at this moment.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#set-a-users-credit-balance)

Set a user's credit balance


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action uses the [Stripe Customer API](https://stripe.com/docs/api/customers)
 to set an absolute value for a customer's balance.

**Note:** This action does the same thing the legacy action "Adjust a user's balance" did. It has been renamed to more accurately reflect the action it performs - setting an absolute value rather than adjusting an existing value.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#user-2)

User

Define the user that corresponds to the Stripe customer whose balance you'd like to set. The user must already be a Stripe customer in your system.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#amount-3)

Amount

Specify the absolute value of the above user's balance. For example, setting the value to $0 will wipe out any positive (amount owed) or negative (amount credited) value for the current customer balance.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#adjust-a-users-credit-balance)

Adjust a user's credit balance


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action uses the [Stripe Credit Balance API](https://stripe.com/docs/api/customer_balance_transactions/create)
 to increase (debit) or decrease (credit) a user's Credit Balance. This action can be used to issue a credit to a user that will be documented as a Transaction on their account and applied to their next invoice. See [Stripe Documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/billing/customer/balance)
 for more detail.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#user-3)

User

Define the user that corresponds to the Stripe customer whose balance you'd like to adjust. The user must already be a Stripe customer in your system.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#type-of-adjustment)

Type of adjustment

Select the type of adjustment you would like to issue to the above user. The options are **credit**, meaning you owe them money, or **debit,** meaning they owe you money.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#amount-4)

Amount

Specify the amount you would like to credit or debit from the user's Stripe credit balance, depending of the type of adjustment you selected above.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#currency-4)

Currency

Specify the currency (USD, EUR, etc.) of the amount. This currency must match the one set by the customer. If the customer’s currency is not set, it will be updated to this value.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#transfer-to-seller)

Transfer to seller


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action uses the [Stripe Transfer API](https://stripe.com/docs/api/transfers/create)
 to send funds from your Stripe account to a Connected (seller) account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#amount-5)

Amount

Enter the amount to send. It can be dynamic using the 'Insert dynamic data' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#currency-5)

Currency

Enter the currency of the transaction. If the currency is not in the list you can type it.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#destination-seller)

Destination seller

Enter the User that the money will be sent to. Note, the user must be a registered seller with a valid Connected account for the transfer to be valid.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#description-4)

Description

Optionally enter a description of the Transfer to be shown on the record of the transaction.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#source-charge-id)

Source charge ID

Optionally enter the pre-authorized charge ID as the source of this transfer instead of the parent Stripe account balance.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#transfer-group)

Transfer group

Optionally enter a text identifier for this individual transaction as a part of a group of transactions. More information on transfer groups can be found in the [Stripe Connect Documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/connect/charges-transfers#transfer-options)
.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#create-payout)

Create payout


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action sends a payout in the amount specified from your Stripe account balance to the bank account linked to your Stripe account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#amount-6)

Amount

Enter the amount to send. It can be dynamic using the 'Insert dynamic data' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#currency-6)

Currency

Enter the currency of the transaction. If the currency is not in the list you can type it.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#enter-api-key)

Enter API Key

This button will appear if not all of your Live keys ([Client ID](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#live-client-id)
, [Secret Key](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#live-secret-key)
, [Publishable Key](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#live-publishable-key)
) are filled out in the Stripe plugin settings. Clicking this button will bring you to the plugin settings page so you can fill them out. Refer to the [Setup](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#setup)
 section below for more information on these keys.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#create-a-stripe-value-list-item)

**Create a Stripe value list item**


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This action creates a new [Value List Item](https://stripe.com/docs/api/radar/value_list_items)
 object, which is added to the specified parent value list.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#value-list-id)

**Value list ID**

Enter the identifier of the value list which the created item will be added to.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#value)

**Value**

Enter the value of the item.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#enter-api-key-1)

Enter API Key

This button will appear if not all of your Live keys (Client ID, Secret Key, Publishable Key) are filled out in the Stripe plugin settings. Clicking this button will bring you to the plugin settings page so you can fill them out. Refer to the [Setup section](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#setup)
 for more information on these keys.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#manage-tax-ids)

Manage Tax IDs


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#create-a-tax-id)

Create a Tax ID

*   Action
    
    *   Creates a tax ID in Stripe associated with the current user.
        
    
*   Fields:
    
    *   type: required; type of the tax id. (The available options are [here](https://stripe.com/docs/api/customer_tax_ids/object#tax_id_object-type)
        )
        
    *   value: required; value of the tax id. (string)
        
    

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#delete-a-tax-id)

Delete a Tax ID

*   Action
    
    *   Deletes a tax ID associated with the current user. If the tax ID doesn’t exist, we do nothing.
        
    
*   Fields:
    
    *   Tax ID: required; the identifier of this tax ID. (string)
        
    

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#stripe-tax-ids)

Stripe Tax IDs

*   Action
    
    *   Returns a list of all of the current user’s tax IDs.
        
    
*   Fields:
    
    *   Limit: optional; max is 5 per customer
        
    

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#stripe-tax-ids-1)

Stripe Tax IDs

*   User data call (i.e., Current User’s Stripe Tax IDs)
    
    *   Returns a list of all of the current user’s tax IDs.
        
    
*   Fields:
    
    *   Limit: optional; max is 5 per customer
        
    

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#retrieve-tax-id)

Retrieve Tax ID

*   External data call
    
    *   Returns a tax ID
        
    
*   Fields:
    
    *   Tax ID: required; the identifier of this tax ID. (string)
        
    

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#setup)

Setup


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You will need an active Stripe account before you can set up the Stripe plugin. Go to [Stripe](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/www.stripe.com)
 to create an account.

**Note:** Stripe has two environments (like Bubble) Live and Test. If both environment keys are set below, your Bubble app will use the development (test) keys in development and the Live keys in Live.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#live-client-id)

Live Client ID

Enter the Client ID from the Stripe Live environment. This information can be found in the Stripe Dashboard > Settings > Connect settings.

**Note:** This key is only relevant if you will be using Stripe Connect functionality.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#live-secret-key)

Live Secret Key

Enter your Secret Key from the Stripe Live environment. This information can be found in the Stripe Dashboard > Developers > API Keys settings.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#live-publishable-key)

Live Publishable Key

Enter your Publishable Key from the Stripe Live environment. This information can be found in the Stripe Dashboard > Developers > API Keys settings.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#client-id-development)

Client ID - development

Enter the Client ID from the Stripe Test environment. This information can be found in the Stripe Dashboard > Settings > Connect settings.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#secret-key-development)

Secret Key - development

Enter your Secret Key from the Stripe Test environment. This information can be found in the Stripe Dashboard > Developers > API Keys settings.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#publishable-key-development)

Publishable Key - development

Enter your Publishable Key from the Stripe Test environment. This information can be found in the Stripe Dashboard > Developers > API Keys settings.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#image-for-stripe-checkout)

Image for Stripe Checkout

Add a default image to be included in your Stripe Checkout sessions.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#name-for-stripe-checkout)

Name for Stripe Checkout

Enter a default product name that will appear in the checkout flow and in the email users receive from Stripe.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#stripe-checkout-version)

Stripe Checkout Version

Select the version of the Stripe Plugin that you will like to use.

**Note:** v3 is strongly recommended as v2 relies on an outdated version of Stripe Checkout and is no longer supported.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#enable-link-on-checkout-v3)

Enable Link on Checkout (v3)

This enables Link payments on any Stripe Checkout page that the user is directed to. If a user completes a transaction using Link, then that same payment method will be used for future transactions executed by the "Charge a user using saved CC" action.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#collect-the-users-address-with-checkout)

Collect the user's address with Checkout

Check this box if you would like to collect the user's address when collecting a customer's payment information during a Checkout session.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#faq)

FAQ


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#can-i-use-a-custom-domain-with-stripe-checkout)

**Can I use a custom domain with Stripe checkout**

Yes, you can choose to use a custom domain with Stripe Checkout and your Bubble app. This means you can send your customers to [checkout.yourbubbleapp.com](http://checkout.yourbubbleapp.com/)
 (or whatever subdomain you want to use) when they are transacting on your app instead of the default [checkout.stripe.com](http://checkout.stripe.com/)
. Note that Stripe charges an extra fee for custom domains.

In order to get a custom domain working, you’ll want to follow the steps listed in [Stripe’s documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/payments/checkout/custom-domains)
, namely:

1.  Adding your domain to the Stripe Dashboard
    
2.  Creating DNS records to point at Stripe
    
3.  Verifying your DNS setup
    
4.  Setting your domain as active
    

On the Bubble side, the Bubble-created official Stripe Plugin supports this feature, so once you’ve set things up on the Stripe and the DNS side, they’ll work in your app as well as long as you are using Checkout v3 within the plugin. The following actions will be affected:

*   Charge the current user
    
*   Subscribe the user to a plan
    
*   Collect the user’s CC information
    

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#stripe-and-privacy-rules)

**Stripe and Privacy Rules**

Privacy rules currently bundle certain fields under “Social Networks,” including Stripe customer data. If you would like to get the customer ID and subscription information, this field will need to be enabled. This privacy rule controls plugin-borne fields, most commonly social network signup fields. When you sign up with an OAuth provider like Facebook, for example, this controls your Facebook name and profile picture.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#stripe-and-iframes-v2)

Stripe and iFrames (v2)

By default, iFrames are blocked for your Bubble application, which will cause the Stripe v2 checkout to navigate to a broken page on mobile if this setting is not updated.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#android-webview-v2)

Android Webview (v2)

The webview browser on Android is not supported by Stripe’s legacy Checkout (v2), which can make it fail. Given that the webview controls its own user agent, however, you can “pretend” that your browser is another one by setting your webview’s user agent to another browser’s, for example:

*   Desktop Chrome
    

`webView.getSettings().setUserAgentString("any user agent string");`

*   iOS 9 webview
    

`webView.getSettings().setUserAgentString("Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 9_3 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/601.1.46 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/9.0 Mobile/13E233 Safari/601.1");`

You can experiment with similar settings at the top of your Android application, if wrapping your Bubble app.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#test-mode-plans-vs-live-mode-plans)

Test Mode Plans vs Live Mode Plans

If both dev and live keys are set, your app will be interacting with Stripe's test environment when you are in development mode, and Stripe's live environment when you deploy to live. If your Stripe plan ids are different between your test mode and live mode, and you are selecting a plan id in dev, this can lead to errors in live as this plan id will not exist in this environment. Consider adjusting your Stripe plans to have matching ids between environments. If this is not possible, try dynamically setting the plan id with `Isn't live version:formatted as text` and set the text value of "yes" to the dev plan id and "no" to the live plan id.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#language-support-v2)

Language Support (v2)

Stripe Plugin version v2 uses an older version of the Stripe Checkout API that has limited language support, including for languages like Portuguese. Consider upgrading to v3 for more robust language support. When an unsupported language is detected, v2 will default to English.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#other-ways-to-learn)

Other ways to learn


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Articles[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#articles)

*   [eCommerce and payments](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/building-your-first-app/ecommerce-and-payments)
     - this article series covers how to understand and plan for different types of payments
    

Other payment gateways with official plugins[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/stripe#other-payment-gateways-with-official-plugins)

*   [Braintree plugin](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubble-made-plugins/braintree)

# General properties | Bubble Docs

Experience level

In-depth articles (11)

Videos (1 playlist)

This core reference entry is suited for **beginner-level builders****.**

Learn more about experience levels.

To learn about this topic more in-depth, we recommend reading the suggested articles below:

**Tools**

*   Article: [The element property editor](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/tabs-and-sections/design-tab/the-property-editor)
    

* * *

**Elements** In this article series, we cover how to work with different element types:

*   Article series: [Elements](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements)
    
    *   Article series: [The element hierarchy](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements/the-element-hierarchy)
        
    *   Article: [The page](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements/web-app/the-page)
        
    *   Article series: [Container elements](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements/web-app/containers)
         Elements that contain other elements.
        
    *   Article: [Visual elements](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements/web-app/visual-elements)
         Elements like text, buttons, icons and images.
        
    *   Article series: [Input forms](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements/web-app/input-forms)
         Elements that accept input, such as text and file uploads.
        
    
*   Article: [Conditional expressions](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/logic/conditions)
     Making your elements change appearance in response to varying conditions.
    

* * *

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#design)

Design

Article series focusing on design in general, explaining terminology and offering resources to help you set up a user-friendly, good looking design.

*   Article series: [Design](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design)
    
    *   Article: [Responsive design](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/responsive-design)
         Building pages that work on all devices, such as a laptop and a phone.
        
    
*   Article: ´[The design tab](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/tabs-and-sections/design-tab)
    

* * *

**Previewing your app**

In this section about how to preview your app in the development environment.

Article: [Previewing your app](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/previewing-your-app)

Bubble Academy: [Element videos](https://www.youtube.com/@BubbleIO/search?query=element)
 (list of videos related to elements)

The properties here apply to **all** **elements** in Bubble.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#changing-the-parent-elements-type-of-content)

Changing the parent element's type of content


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This option modifies the type of content of the parent element on the fly. The type of content defines what type of thing a group should expect, so that you can refer to it when editing the app. For example, a group can receive a user, who isn't necessarily the current user, and then you can add a text element in the group to display the 'Parent group's user's email.'

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#this-element-is-visible-on-page-load)

This element is visible on page load


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Check this box to make the element visible by default. This checkbox makes the element visible every time the page loads or reloads. Change the visibility of the element based on certain conditions in the Conditional section in the Property Editor or with a show/hide element action in a workflow. To find hidden elements quickly, click the 'Only show hideable' checkbox in the Elements tree in the Palette. Clicking on the name shows or hides the element for editing purposes only. Like other properties, this setting can be overwritten by a condition on an element or by a show/hide action in a workflow. Actions take precedence over conditions which take precedence over the default setting.

**Note on security:** Be aware that tech-savvy users can access the underlying static content of any page, even if certain elements are hidden from their view. As such, never place sensitive information such as private URLs or tokens in hidden elements on the page.

You can read more about maintaining page security in our dedicated article below:

User manual article: [Page security](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/security/page-security)

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#replace-element)

Replace element


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Click this button to replace an element with an element of a different type. For example, change an element from a [checkbox](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/input-forms#checkbox)
 to a [radio button](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/input-forms#radio-buttons)
 or from a [group](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#group)
 to a [popup](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#popup)
.

Elements can only be replaced by elements within their own [category](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements)
. For example, a [text input element](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/input-forms#input)
 [(input forms](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/input-forms)
 category) cannot be replaced by a [group](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers#group)
 ([container](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers)
 category).

This operation may lead to inconsistencies, so check the Issues Checker to make sure no new issues are introduced. If you are experiencing issue, you can use Undo to revert the change.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#style)

Style


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Select a style from this dropdown menu to apply to the selected element. Styles are defined in the Styles Tab and can be applied to more than one element. When a style is modified, all elements with that style will automatically change. Using styles makes editing the app faster and ensures a consistent design.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#detach-style)

Detach style


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clicking this button detaches the style from the selected element. The style association is detached, but the properties of the style remain on the element.

The style is not changed or deleted, only detached from the specific element.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#edit-style)

Edit style


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clicking this button displays the Styles Tab and selects the current element's style.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#override-style)

Override style


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To override a style, change one of its values. The style dropdown will show an _(overriden)._

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#reset)

Reset

To reset the elements style back to the selected style, click _Reset_ next to _Appearance settings_. This button is only visible when the style is overriden.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#notes-on-resetting-styles)

Notes on resetting styles

A few notes on the behavior of Style overrides:

*   Resetting will change any overridden values on a tab by tab basis
    
*   If you override any values, and then update the attached Style, the overridden values will not change - even if those settings on the Style were updated
    
*   If you override any values, and then detach the Style, the overridden values will not change
    
*   If you override any values, and then detach the Style, and then reattach a Style, the overridden values will be removed in favor of the new Style’s inherited values.
    
*   If you override any values, and then change the overridden value back to the original value, the value will still be considered an override. To “re-attach” the value, use the reset button
    
*   If you override any values, and then create a new Style from the element, the overridden values will carry over to the new Style
    
*   Conditionals cannot be overridden yet. If you want to override an inherited conditional, simply add a new style that has the behavior you want since conditionals are evaluated from top to bottom
    

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#select-parent)

Select parent


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This dropdown shows all the parent elements of the element you are currently editing.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#reveal-in-elements-tree)

Reveal in Elements Tree


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clicking this button shows where the element is in the Elements tree.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#lock-this-element-not-draggable-in-editor)

Lock this element (not draggable in editor)


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When this box is checked, the element will not be draggable in the Bubble editor. This is useful when working on large pages. This has no impact in run-mode.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#id-attribute-advanced)

ID Attribute (advanced)


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Note: This is an advanced feature best suited for those with at least a basic understanding of CSS.

If you activate the option _Expose the option to add an ID attribute to HTML elements_ in the Settings Tab, you can specific a unique ID for some element on the page. This ID will be applied in run mode to the outer div around the element. **Warning**: Bubble does not guarantee that the internal structure of the divs will remain the same over time. What is guaranteed here is the ID will be applied to the outer element. Keep this mind as you use IDs.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#margins)

Margins


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Margins create extra space **around** an element. Margins define the overall size of a visual element on the page and how it interacts with elements around it in both fixed and responsive layouts.

For example, in a fixed container layout, margins can be used to provide even spacing around elements in a form. In a row container layout, margins will be added to the min and max width of the element to inform when the element should wrap to the next row.

In addition, margins will align and snap based on the margin border, not the element border. If the margin is 0, the margin border and element border will be the same.

Margin controls are available on all elements in the Layout tab of the property editor. These controls are also available in conditional statements to replace the legacy responsive “collapse margins” functionality.

**Tip:** In a responsive container, margins will help you position a child element where you want it once you have selected the appropriate alignment option. For example, if you have a button in a **column** container and you want the button to be 30px from the bottom of the container, simply **bottom-align** the button and add 30px of margin to the bottom of the button element.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F34394582-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F-M5sbzwG7CljeZdkntrL%252Fuploads%252F14abNVK9GTpoTw0pBllm%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd6ceccb2-edf0-403d-86ac-ff96d60297a5&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=36142d03&sv=2)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#margin-top)

Top

Specify the amount of margin at the top of the element in pixels.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#margin-right)

Right

Specify the amount of margin on the right side of the element in pixels.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#margin-bottom)

Bottom

Specify the amount of margin to the bottom of the element in pixels.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#margin-left)

Left

Specify the amount of margin on the left side of the element in pixels.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#padding)

Padding


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Padding creates extra space **within** an element. In general, padding defines the amount of "usable" space inside of an element. Since new padding controls are only available on Containers (for now), padding will define how close to the edge of a parent container a child element is allowed to get.

For example, in a Column container layout, a padding of 30px on the left and ride side of the container will add 30px of space inside the container on each side. If the Parent container's width is set to 420 px, the largest width any child element could have is 360px - (420px - (30px \*2)).

If a parent container has padding applied, child elements will align or snap on the boundaries of the padding area, not the container border.

Similar to margins, padding controls are also available in conditional statements and can be saved in Styles.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F34394582-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F-M5sbzwG7CljeZdkntrL%252Fuploads%252F0Ka5brvZVX1Ja8DmnHqt%252Fimage%2520%281%29.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D3a8d0189-2b33-48bc-883f-62ffef3a77f0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=51588901&sv=2)

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#padding-top)

Top

Specify the amount of padding at the top of the container in pixels.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#padding-right)

Right

Specify the amount of padding on the right side of the container in pixels.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#padding-bottom)

Bottom

Specify the amount of padding to the bottom of the container in pixels.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#padding-left)

Left

Specify the amount of padding on the left side of the container in pixels.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#edit-style-1)

Style / Style (overridden)

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F34394582-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F-M5sbzwG7CljeZdkntrL%252Fuploads%252FeHdIHK9oytAlgDjR6Cjo%252Fstyle-padding.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dad7f8a3d-12a6-42c3-9d9f-a55410289734&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=436a6fe3&sv=2)

If padding is applied to this element from a Style, click this link to navigate to the Styles tab to make edits to the Style as needed. If you have made changes to the padding that differ from the style, the link will say _Style (overridden)_. You can reset to the style's setting using the [_Reset_ link](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties#reset)
 on the right-hand side.

If you don't have a style applied, this setting will not be visible.

Last updated 1 year ago

Was this helpful?

---

# General properties (Legacy) | Bubble Docs

The properties here apply to all elements in Bubble.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties-1#changing-the-parent-elements-type)

Changing the parent element's type


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This option modifies the type of content of the parent element on the fly. The type of content defines what type of thing a group should expect, so that you can refer to it when editing the app. For example, a group can receive a user, who isn't necessarily the current user, and then you can add a text element in the group to display the 'Parent group's user's email.'

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties-1#this-element-is-visible-on-page-load)

This element is visible on page load


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Check this box to make the element visible by default. This checkbox makes the element visible every time the page loads or reloads. Change the visibility of the element based on certain conditions in the Conditional section in the Property Editor or with a show/hide element action in a workflow. To find hidden elements quickly, click the 'Only show hideable' checkbox in the Elements tree in the Palette. Clicking on the name shows or hides the element for editing purposes only. Like other properties, this setting can be overwritten by a condition on an element or by a show/hide action in a workflow. Actions take precedence over conditions which take precedence over the default setting.

Note that savvy end-users can 'see' (through technical means) the static content of any page they visit, even if the elements are hidden to that end-user and never revealed. This means you should not include any sensitive static content in hidden elements on the page - content like sensitive URLs or private tokens.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties-1#replace-element)

Replace element


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Click this button to replace an element with an element of a different type. For example, change an element from a checkbox to a toggle or from a group to a popup. Note: This operation may lead to inconsistencies, so check the Issues Checker to make sure no new issues are introduced. Undo this change, if necessary.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties-1#style)

Style


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Select a style from this dropdown menu to apply to the selected element. Styles are defined in the Styles Tab and can be applied to more than one element. When a style is modified, all elements with that style will automatically change. Using styles makes editing the app faster and ensures a consistent design.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties-1#reset)

Reset


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clicking the Reset link will re-apply the padding from the element's style. The link is only visible if you have made changes to the padding.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties-1#edit-style)

Edit style


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clicking this button displays the Styles Tab and selects the current element's style.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties-1#select-parent)

Select parent


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Choose an element from this dropdown menu to contain the selected element. This is convenient when an element is contained by a group with the same dimensions.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties-1#reveal-in-elements-tree)

Reveal in Elements Tree


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clicking this button shows where the element is in the Elements tree in the Palette.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties-1#lock-this-element-not-draggable-in-editor)

Lock this element (not draggable in editor)


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When this box is checked, the element will not be draggable in the Bubble Editor. This is useful when working on large pages. This has no impact in run mode.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/shared-properties-1#id-attribute-advanced)

ID Attribute (advanced)


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you activate the option Expose the option to add an ID attribute to HTML elements in the Settings Tab, you can specific a unique ID for some element on the page. This ID will be applied in run mode to the outer div around the element. This is an advanced feature. Warning: Bubble does not guarantee that the internal structure of the divs will remain the same over time. What is guaranteed here is the ID will be applied to the outer element. Keep this mind as you use IDs.

Last updated 1 year ago

Was this helpful?
