# What Plugins Can Do | Bubble Docs

Plugins are elements, actions, events or data sources that are not part of the Bubble core, but that you can use in your application. A plugin can add different things to your app, or a combination of these things.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/what-plugins-can-do#background-services)

Background services


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A plugin can add some code in the headers of your page and enable some background services in your app. Analytics services, for instance, are a very common background service that can be added to an app (Google Analytics, etc.).

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/what-plugins-can-do#api-data-sources)

API data sources


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Plugins can add API connections that can be used in the 'Get data from API' datasource. Getting songs from iTunes, fetching animated GIFs from Giphy are examples of API Data sources.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/what-plugins-can-do#authentication-services)

Authentication services


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A plugin can add an authentication method to your app, through OAuth. OAuth (Open Authorization) is an open standard for token-based authentication and authorization on the Internet. It allows an end user's account information to be used by third-party services, such as Facebook, without exposing the user's password. Practically, you'll be able to add a button to your app 'Login with Facebook', and users, instead of typing a password in your app, will authorize Facebook to grant access to your app.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/what-plugins-can-do#elements)

Elements


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Many elements in Bubble are coming from plugins. Bubble's core elements only offer most common elements, as soon as you want to add something specific to your app, like a slideshow, an Excel-like table, etc. plugins will be the way to find these elements. Practically, these elements will function and behave similarly to Bubble's core elements in run-mode. Note that in edit mode, Community-built elements are not rendered, and will be displayed with a placeholder image.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/what-plugins-can-do#actions)

Actions


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Plugins can add actions that connect with external services to your app. For instance, post something on the user's Facebook wall, send an email with a different provider, charge a credit card, etc. These actions behave in a similar manner as core Bubble actions.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/what-plugins-can-do#private-plugins)

Private plugins


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Plugins can be used to extend Bubble with your custom functionality as well. If you need some specific code to run, connect to a company's backend, you can write your own plugins and add them to your app.

Last updated 4 years ago

Was this helpful?

---

# Toolbar | Bubble Docs

Experience level

In-depth articles (7)

This core reference entry is suited for **beginner-level builders.**

Learn more about experience levels.

To learn about this topic more in-depth, we recommend reading the suggested articles below:

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#bubble-editor-tools)

Bubble editor tools

This article series covers the different tools available in the Bubble editor.

*   Article series: [Bubble editor tools](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/tools)
    
    *   Article section: [The app interface manager](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/tools/key-features#app-interface-manager)
        
    *   Article: [The search tool](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/tools/the-search-tool)
        
    *   Article: [The Issue Checker](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/tools/the-issue-checker)
        
    *   Article: [The element tree](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/design/elements/the-element-hierarchy/the-element-tree)
        
    *   Article: [The element property editor](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/tabs-and-sections/design-tab/the-property-editor)
        
    *   Article: [The debugger](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/maintaining-an-application/testing-and-debugging/using-the-debugger)
        
    *   Article: [Notes](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/tools/notes)
        
    

The toolbar contains many of Bubble's essential features. This section will explain each tool from left to right. Keep in mind that the tools displayed in the toolbar may change depending on the tab you are currently viewing.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#the-bubble-logo-main-menu)

The Bubble logo / main menu


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#back-to-my-apps)

Back to my Apps

This option takes you back to the dashboard of Bubble apps connected to your account.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#edit)

Edit

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#undo)

Undo

Undo the last change.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#redo)

Redo

Redo the last change.

**Clipboard:** Bubble uses an internal clipboard that operates separately from your computer’s operating system clipboard. For example, when you copy an element using the Bubble editor's built-in copy feature, it won’t overwrite any content already stored on your OS clipboard (such as text).

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#cut)

Cut

Copies and deletes the selected component(s), such as elements on the page or workflow events/actions. Workflows associated with cut elements are not included. See [_copy with workflows_](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#copy-with-workflows)
.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#copy)

Copy

Copies the selected component(s), such as elements on the page or workflow events/actions. Workflows associated with cut elements are not included. See [_copy with workflows_](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#copy-with-workflows)
.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#copy-with-workflows)

Copy with workflows

Copies the selected element(s) and any workflow(s) that are triggered by those element(s).

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#copy-formatting)

Copy formatting

Copies the formatting of the currently selected element.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#copy-layout-settings)

Copy layout settings

Copies the layout settings of the currently selected element (such as margins, padding and size).

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#copy-conditionals)

Copy conditionals

Copies the conditions stored on the currently selected element.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#copy-to-another-app)

Copy to another app

Use this feature if you are copying one or more elements or workflows to a different app than the one you are currently viewing.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#paste)

Paste

Paste the components currently stored in the Bubble clipboard.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#paste-with-workflows)

Paste with workflows

Paste the components currently stored in the Bubble clipboard.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#paste-formatting)

Paste formatting

Paste the element formatting currently stored in the Bubble clipboard to the currently selected element.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#paste-layout-settings)

Paste layout settings

Paste the element layout settings currently stored in the Bubble clipboard to the currently selected element.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#paste-conditionals)

Paste conditionals

Paste the element conditions currently stored in the Bubble clipboard to the currently selected element.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#delete)

Delete

Delete the currently selected component.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#select-all)

Select all

Select all the elements on the current page.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#select-first-parent)

Select first parent

Select the parent of the currently selected element.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#convert-to-reusable)

Convert to reusable

Creates a reusable element from the currently selected element(s). Note that the original elements are not deleted or replaced by the newly created reusable element. This action navigates away from the current page and into the created resuable element editor.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#detach-reusable)

Detach reusable

Converts the selected reusable element into individual elements, detaching them from the original reusable. This action replaces the reusable on the page with its contents but does not delete the original reusable element from your app.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#make-index)

Make index

Sets the currently open page as your app's new index page. The original index page will be renamed.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#duplicate-page)

Duplicate page

Creates a copy of the currently open page. You will be asked to provide a name for the new page before the operation page is duplicated.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#arrange)

Arrange

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#group-groups-the-currently-selected-element-s-into-a-group-container-element)

**Group** Groups the currently selected element(s) into a group container element.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#ungroup)

**Ungroup**

Removes the selected elements from their group, turning them back into individual elements.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#bring-to-front)

**Bring to front**

Moves the selected element(s) to the top layer, placing them above all other overlapping elements.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#send-to-back)

**Send to back**

Moves the selected element(s) to the bottom layer, placing them behind all other overlapping elements.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#align-left)

**Align left**

Aligns the selected element(s) to the left edge of the furthest left element in the selection.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#align-horizontal-centers)

**Align horizontal centers**

Aligns the selected element(s) so that their horizontal centers are aligned.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#align-right)

**Align right**

Aligns the selected element(s) to the right edge of the furthest right element in the selection.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#align-top)

**Align top**

Aligns the selected element(s) to the top edge of the highest element in the selection.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#align-vertical-centers)

**Align vertical centers**

Aligns the selected element(s) so that their vertical centers are aligned.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#align-bottom)

**Align bottom**

Aligns the selected element(s) to the bottom edge of the lowest element in the selection.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#center-horizontally)

**Center horizontally**

Centers the selected element(s) horizontally within the parent container.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#center-vertically)

**Center vertically**

Centers the selected element(s) vertically within the parent container.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#distribute-horizontally)

**Distribute horizontally**

Evenly spaces the selected element(s) across the horizontal axis, based on their outer edges.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#distribute-vertically)

**Distribute vertically**

Evenly spaces the selected element(s) along the vertical axis, based on their outer edges.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#views)

Views

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#x-ray-mode)

**X-ray mode**

Displays all elements on the page with transparent backgrounds, making it easier to view and select overlapping or hidden elements.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#lock-property-editor)

**Lock Property Editor**

When this option is on, the property editor pane will be locked on the right side of the design canvas instead of being draggable.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#element-borders)

**Element borders**

Outlines all elements on the page with visible borders, helping to distinguish and align elements more easily.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#element-thumbnails)

**Element thumbnails**

Shows small preview images of elements when associated atoms or workflow components are hovered, providing a quick visual reference.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#element-labels)

**Element labels**

Displays the names of elements directly on the page, making it easier to identify and manage different components.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#snap-elements-to-edges)

**Snap elements to edges**

Automatically aligns elements to the edges of nearby elements for precise positioning.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#snap-elements-to-grid)

**Snap elements to grid**

Aligns elements to the closest point on the grid, ensuring consistent spacing and alignment.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#snap-to-edges-and-grid)

**Snap to edges and grid**

Combines both edge and grid snapping, allowing elements to align with nearby elements and the grid simultaneously.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#do-not-snap-elements)

**Do not snap elements**

Disables snapping, allowing for freeform placement of elements without automatic alignment.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#grids-and-guides)

Grids and guides

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#show-grid)

**Show grid**

Displays a grid overlay on the editor canvas to help with aligning and positioning elements.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#show-horizontal-lines)

**Show horizontal lines**

Adds horizontal guide lines to the grid for better vertical alignment (disabled in the image).

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#step-grid)

**Step (grid)**

Defines the spacing between grid lines. A larger number increases the distance between lines.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#color-grid)

**Color (grid)**

Sets the color of the grid lines for better visibility against different backgrounds.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#include-gutters)

**Include gutters**

Adds spacing (gutters) between grid columns to help structure content layout.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#start-with-gutter)

**Start with gutter**

Begins the grid layout with a gutter, ensuring consistent spacing from the page edge.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#step-gutter)

**Step (gutter)**

Sets the width of the gutter space between grid columns.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#color-gutter)

**Color (gutter)**

Adjusts the color of the gutter lines for visual clarity.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#show-distances-on-hover)

**Show distances on hover**

Displays the distance between elements when hovering over them in the editor for precise spacing adjustments.

#### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#color-hover-distance)

**Color (hover distance)**

Determines the color of the distance indicators shown on hover.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#app-interface-manager)

App Interface Manager


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The app interface manager lets you first:

*   Switch between your web app and native mobile app.
    
*   Navigate the different pages/views and reusable elements associated with each app type-
    

Search your application for pages, native mobile views and reusable elements and native mobile views. You can also organize pages, native mobile views and reusable elements into folders.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#version-control-selector)

Version control selector


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Opens up the version control panel.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#save-status)

Save status


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Displays the status of your recent changes to indicate whether they have been saved. If Bubble is unable to save your app—such as during an internet connection issue—a notification will alert you to the error.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#issue-checker)

Issue checker


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Shows the current number of issues, as tracked by the [issue checker](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/tools/the-issue-checker)
.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#search-tool)

Search tool


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Opens up the app [search tool](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/getting-started/navigating-the-bubble-editor/tools/the-search-tool)
.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#preview)

Preview


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generates a preview of your app and opens it in a new tab in your browser. Use the arrow down symbol for more options.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#preview-1)

Preview

Preview the app with no features disabled. Same as clicking the play symbol.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#disable-custom-html)

Disable custom HTML

Preview the app with custom HTML disabled.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#disable-plugins)

Disable plugins

Preview the app with plugins disabled.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#disable-html-and-plugins)

Disable HTML and plugins

Preview the app with plugins and custom HTML disabled.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#deploy)

Deploy


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Displays the deploy panel. Note that you must have 0 issues in the issue checker to be able to deploy.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#undo-1)

Undo


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Undo the last change.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#redo-1)

Redo


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Redo the last undone change.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#page-canvas-toolbar-options)

Page canvas toolbar options


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This section shows toolbar options specific for the page editor canvas

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#inline-element-search)

Inline element search

Search the current page for elements by name.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/bubbles-interface/toolbar#zoom)

Zoom

Zoom in/out or to 100%.

**Zoom:** Bubble’s zoom feature operates separately from the browser’s zoom. This means that only the page and its elements are scaled in or out, while the rest of the editor—such as toolbars, menus, and other tools—remains unaffected. Using the browser’s built-in zoom is not recommended, as it may lead to unexpected behavior while editing.

Last updated 2 months ago

Was this helpful?

# Containers (Legacy) | Bubble Docs

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#group)

Group


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Groups are elements that contain other elements. A group has two roles. First, a group gathers elements from a visual perspective, and all the elements in that group move together. It also has a semantic significance. A group can have data injected into it, and the elements inside the group can refer to the Parent group's thing. For example, you can 'display' a user in a group, and all elements inside that group will be able to display the 'Parent group's user's email.' Use this functionality when you want some areas of the app's page to display items relative to a dynamic entry.

Our Academy quick tip on how to group elements together

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#type-of-content)

Type of content

This allows you to select the type of thing this container displays. Most elements that have this property also have a Data source property, which lets you select the displayed thing or list of things. The value of the data source must match the selected type of content. Let's look at an example of a map with hotels marked. When you click a marker, a popup displays with the name of the hotel. The Type of content would be 'Hotel' and the Data source would be 'Map's current marker.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#data-source)

Data source

Define the thing or list of things that should be displayed in this container. For example, in groups or popups, only a single thing will display, but for repeating groups a list of things will display. Child elements can access the Data source from their parent. If the parent contains a list of things, the child has access to the individual list items. A common scenario for groups is to display something, like the current user, and then have the group contain the inputs that modify the user's properties, such as text and images that display information about the user. The Type of content determines what kind of thing you are allowed to set in the Data source field. If the type of thing does not match the Data source, it will be marked as an issue, and you must change one or the other to match. The Data source can come from anywhere in Bubble. For example, it could be the result of a search, a custom or built-in Data source, or it could be dynamic and come from the user. The Data source will update when its source changes. For example, if the search result changes because a user modified some data used in the search constraints, the displayed list will automatically change. For additional flexibility, you can update the Data source in the Conditional Formatting Tab in the Property Editor. Finally, the Data source property can be modified by workflows. Changes made by actions override whatever you specify as the Data source directly or as a conditional state until you run a reset action, which then restores the original setting.

**Note:** As mentioned, Groups can have data assigned to them (e.g. 'this group should show data about user X'). When the data assigned to a group changes, the group will reset all the input fields within itself.

This is useful when the input fields are supposed to change depending on the group's data. But, it can also cause confusion, for example, when a group's data depends on an input that's inside that group!

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#collapse-this-elements-height-when-hidden)

Collapse this element's height when hidden

By default, hiding an element leaves a blank space on the page, exposing whatever is underneath it. When you select this option, however, the elements below the hidden element are pulled upward to fill the space.

**Tip:** A common pattern for a messaging UI involves a Repeating Group where cells have two Groups (Sender & Receiver) that display the text sent by each respective party. When the message belongs to the Sender, the Receiver group is hidden and vice versa.

Because of a race condition between evaluating conditional visibility of groups in Repeating Group cells and evaluating cell height, blank spaces may appear when switching between the two groups when a new message is sent.

To minimize the risk of this happening, consider enabling "Collapse this element's height when hidden" on both Groups.

**Note:** the "collapse when hidden" property may not always work as expected when used on groups that overlap other groups using the setting.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#animate-the-collapse-operation)

Animate the collapse operation

This option is only visible if 'Collapse this element's height when hidden' is selected. By default, elements collapse and expand instantly, but this option animates with either Fade In/Out or Slide Up/Down.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#animation-style)

Animation style

This option allows you to choose whether an element's animation will Fade In/Out or Slide Up/Down.

**Note:** Animation Style is not currently supported for use on a group in situations where a repeating group's underlying data is changing. In these situations, consider relying on the repeating group's built-in updating instead of adding an animation.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#start-edit-workflow)

Start/Edit workflow

Click this button to create a new workflow triggered by this element. The type of event depends on the element and is generally the most common event. For example, buttons will create a 'When this element is clicked...' event. If a workflow already exists, this takes you to the existing workflow in the Workflow Tab. To create a different type of event or create a second event, go to the Workflow Tab and click the 'Click here to add an event...' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#this-element-isnt-clickable)

This element isn't clickable

When checked, the element will not trigger a workflow when clicked nor will the user's cursor change to a pointer when they hover over the element. In addition, the item will display according to any specified conditional formatting for the 'isn't clickable' state. This can be used to stop a user from moving forward in a flow until some condition is true. For example, we might disable a specific action until the user is logged in. 'This input should not be empty' can also be used to prevent the user from clicking.

**Tip:** Conditionally changing the borders of a Group will not resize the content in the group, which can lead to undesirable formatting. Consider using two groups (one without a border), and toggling between the two if this behavior is desired.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#repeating-group)

Repeating Group


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Watch this video to learn about using the Repeating Group Element

A repeating group displays lists of things, either coming from the application database or from APIs. Design the first cell, and the remaining cells will follow that design.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#type-of-content-1)

Type of content

This allows you to select the type of thing this container displays. Most elements that have this property also have a Data source property, which lets you select the displayed thing or list of things. The value of the data source must match the selected type of content. Let's look at an example of a map with hotels marked. When you click a marker, a popup displays with the name of the hotel. The Type of content would be 'Hotel' and the Data source would be 'Map's current marker.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#data-source-1)

Data source

This is the list of things to display in the group. It can either be – The result of a search using 'Do a search for,' – The content of a field whose type is list of things, or – The result of an API call through the 'Get data from an external API.' Once this is set, each cell will display one thing, and the elements drawn inside the cell will be able to refer to the 'Current cell's thing.'

**Note:** Avoid the situation where you automatically change the data source of a repeating group based on a condition that references the repeating group itself! This will cause an infinite loop and the repeating group will not behave as you expect.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#layout-style)

Layout style

Watch our Academy quick tip for examples of each of these layouts

This is where to define the general layout of the repeating group. Note that this has a major impact on the performance of your pages, as it impacts how many items get downloaded to draw the repeating group. There are five possible options: – Vertical scrolling: The element adds cells as the user scrolls down, but it doesn't push the elements below it. Instead, users keep scrolling. This is efficient as it only loads visible items from the server to display the cells. – Extendable vertical scrolling: The element adds cells as the user scrolls down, and it pushes the elements below down. This is like Facebook's feed and is also efficient for the same reason as above. – Full list: The element draws as many cells as there are items in the data source. This is not the best approach when the list is long, as it loads many items, including some that aren't visible at first. This is not recommended for long lists. – Fixed number of cells: Only shows a fixed number of entries. Use the show next/previous actions to navigate in the workflows. The performance depends on your design, with a low number of cells this is efficient, with many cells it can slow down your pages. – Horizontal scrolling: The element add cells as the user scrolls right. In this mode, the repeating group can only have one row. Given the width of screens, this is usually safe performance-wise. The cells are still expandable if the content inside them is 'Set to stretch to fit content.'

**Tip:** The different types of repeating groups behave differently when there is too _little_ content to fill even the drawn number of cells:

*   Vertical scrolling, ext vertical scrolling and fixed number of cells will show, at minimum, the number of cells drawn in the editor, even if there is no content to fill those cells
    
*   A full list repeating group will shrink in height if there's not enough content to fill its cells, potentially even collapsing completely if there is no content
    

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#rows)

Rows

Enter the number of rows to display in this repeating group. This setting only applies when the layout is set to 'Fixed number of cells.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#columns)

Columns

Enter the number of columns to display in this repeating group. Entering 1 will make the element like a list, while a larger number will give a tile aspect to the repeating group.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#cell-minimum-width-for-responsive-px)

Cell minimum width for responsive (px)

When the page is responsive, specify the smallest width the cells can have when the page is resized. When the page becomes too narrow for all the cells to have that width, the repeating group displays fewer cells per row.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#show-partial-list-on-last-page-if-needed)

Show partial list on last page if needed

When the repeating group is set to Fixed number of cells, this controls whether an incomplete last page should be filled with items from the previous page. E.G: If there are 6 items in a 4x1 repeating group, then with this option off, the last page will contain the last four items, items 3 through 6, so items 3 and 4 will be duplicated on both pages; with this option on, the last page will only contain the remaining items, items 5 and 6.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#allow-more-than-one-column-when-stretched)

Allow more than one column when stretched

By default, if a repeating group only has one column, e.g., Excel sheet style, no more columns are added when the page is stretched. To change this behavior, check this box.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#separator-style)

Separator Style

Select the type of border to display between the cells. Choose from None or a stylized line, such as Solid, Dashed, and Double.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#separator-width)

Separator Width

Enter the width to apply to the separator in pixels. Set a width even with a separator of None. In this case, the cells will have some empty space between them.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#separator-color)

Separator Color

Select the separator color.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#start-edit-workflow-1)

Start/Edit workflow

Click this button to create a new workflow triggered by this element. The type of event depends on the element and is generally the most common event. For example, buttons will create a 'When this element is clicked...' event. If a workflow already exists, this takes you to the existing workflow in the Workflow Tab. To create a different type of event or create a second event, go to the Workflow Tab and click the 'Click here to add an event...' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#this-element-isnt-clickable-1)

This element isn't clickable

When checked, the element will not trigger a workflow when clicked nor will the user's cursor change to a pointer when they hover over the element. In addition, the item will display according to any specified conditional formatting for the 'isn't clickable' state. This can be used to stop a user from moving forward in a flow until some condition is true. For example, we might disable a specific action until the user is logged in. 'This input should not be empty' can also be used to prevent the user from clicking.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#popup)

Popup


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Popups are hidden from the user until you display them from the Workflow Tab. When they are visible, they are fixed position in the center of the viewport. If the popup is longer than the viewport, the user will be able to scroll to see the full content. If the popup is shorter than the viewport, the user will not be able to scroll.

Our Academy quick tip on how to use popups

**Tip:** If the popup is longer than the viewport, scrolling will be enabled, and scroll distance is tied to the length of the page in the background. If you have a very long page, the user will be able to continue scrolling the entire length of the background page, potentially past the point where the popup is no longer visible.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#type-of-content-2)

Type of content

This allows you to select the type of thing this container displays. Most elements that have this property also have a Data source property, which lets you select the displayed thing or list of things. The value of the data source must match the selected type of content. Let's look at an example of a map with hotels marked. When you click a marker, a popup displays with the name of the hotel. The Type of content would be 'Hotel' and the Data source would be 'Map's current marker.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#data-source-2)

Data source

Define the thing or list of things that should be displayed in this container. For example, in groups or popups, only a single thing will display, but for repeating groups a list of things will display. Child elements can access the Data source from their parent. If the parent contains a list of things, the child has access to the individual list items. A common scenario for groups is to display something, like the current user, and then have the group contain the inputs that modify the user's properties, such as text and images that display information about the user. The Type of content determines what kind of thing you are allowed to set in the Data source field. If the type of thing does not match the Data source, it will be marked as an issue, and you must change one or the other to match. The Data source can come from anywhere in Bubble. For example, it could be the result of a search, a custom or built-in Data source, or it could be dynamic and come from the user. The Data source will update when its source changes. For example, if the search result changes because a user modified some data used in the search constraints, the displayed list will automatically change. For additional flexibility, you can update the Data source in the Conditional Formatting Tab in the Property Editor. Finally, the Data source property can be modified by workflows. Changes made by actions override whatever you specify as the Data source directly or as a conditional state until you run a reset action, which then restores the original setting.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#this-popup-cant-be-closed-by-pressing-esc)

This popup can't be closed by pressing 'Esc'

By default, popups can be closed by the user when pressing Esc. To disable this behavior and make the popup modal, check this box. In this case, the only way to close the popup is with a Hide Popup action in a workflow.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#grayout-color)

Grayout color

Select the color of the background when a popup is open. By default, it is set to a dark transparent layer.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#grayout-blur)

Grayout blur

Enter a number greater than 0 to set a blur effect on the background. While this offers an interesting design, the display speed of the popup may be affected.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#start-edit-workflow-2)

Start/Edit workflow

Click this button to create a new workflow triggered by this element. The type of event depends on the element and is generally the most common event. For example, buttons will create a 'When this element is clicked...' event. If a workflow already exists, this takes you to the existing workflow in the Workflow Tab. To create a different type of event or create a second event, go to the Workflow Tab and click the 'Click here to add an event...' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#this-element-isnt-clickable-2)

This element isn't clickable

When checked, the element will not trigger a workflow when clicked nor will the user's cursor change to a pointer when they hover over the element. In addition, the item will display according to any specified conditional formatting for the 'isn't clickable' state. This can be used to stop a user from moving forward in a flow until some condition is true. For example, we might disable a specific action until the user is logged in. 'This input should not be empty' can also be used to prevent the user from clicking.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#floating-group)

Floating Group


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Watch our Academy quick tip on how to set up a floating group

Floating groups are groups that float on the screen on top of the page but are not contained within the page. These are useful to keep an element in the same place on the screen regardless of the scrolling position of the page or the visibility of groups within the page. Floating groups are useful for headers, buttons like 'Scroll to top,' etc.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#type-of-content-3)

Type of content

This allows you to select the type of thing this container displays. Most elements that have this property also have a Data source property, which lets you select the displayed thing or list of things. The value of the data source must match the selected type of content. Let's look at an example of a map with hotels marked. When you click a marker, a popup displays with the name of the hotel. The Type of content would be 'Hotel' and the Data source would be 'Map's current marker.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#data-source-3)

Data source

Define the thing or list of things that should be displayed in this container. For example, in groups or popups, only a single thing will display, but for repeating groups a list of things will display. Child elements can access the Data source from their parent. If the parent contains a list of things, the child has access to the individual list items. A common scenario for groups is to display something, like the current user, and then have the group contain the inputs that modify the user's properties, such as text and images that display information about the user. The Type of content determines what kind of thing you are allowed to set in the Data source field. If the type of thing does not match the Data source, it will be marked as an issue, and you must change one or the other to match. The Data source can come from anywhere in Bubble. For example, it could be the result of a search, a custom or built-in Data source, or it could be dynamic and come from the user. The Data source will update when its source changes. For examle, if the search result changes because a user modified some data used in the search constraints, the displayed list will automatically change. For additional flexibility, you can update the Data source in the Conditional Formatting Tab in the Property Editor. Finally, the Data source property can be modified by workflows. Changes made by actions override whatever you specify as the Data source directly or as a conditional state until you run a reset action, which then restores the original setting.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#vertically-float-relative-to)

Vertically float relative to

Using this dropdown menu, choose the base to calculate the position of the floating group. Choose from: – Top: The distance between the top edge of the group and the top of the user's screen remains constant, no matter the scrolling position of the page. This is the default. – Bottom: The distance between the bottom of the group and the user's screen remains constant no matter the scrolling position of the page. – Both: Both the top and bottom distances will remain constant, and a scrollbar will appear if the group is too short to show all its content. – Nothing: The floating group will not float, meaning that it behaves like a regular group. For example, the distance is calculated as the height between the top edge of the floating group and the top of the Bubble page. Then, it displays the floating group that distance from the top of the user's screen. Note: By placing the floating group high on the page and making it float relative to the bottom, the element may be 'above' the top of the screen and not display.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#horizontally-float-relative-to)

Horizontally float relative to

Use this dropdown menu to determine whether the element floats relative to the Right or Left. Right and Left refer to the horizontal edges of the page. This option is less common than vertical reference, but it is useful when you want an element to display no matter the page width.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#floating-zindex)

Floating zindex

Floating groups can be above other elements or beneath the page, and then be used as a background design option. Note that when a floating element is beneath elements, it will only visible if the page background is transparent

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#parallax-effect-factor)

Parallax effect factor

When a floating group is beneath the page, you can apply a parallax effect to give some depth to the page. A factor of 1 will remove any parallax effect, while a factor of 0 will keep the element fixed, and a factor of 2 will make the element move twice faster than the scrolling position. A negative number will have the element move into the different direction of the scroll movement.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#start-edit-workflow-3)

Start/Edit workflow

Click this button to create a new workflow triggered by this element. The type of event depends on the element and is generally the most common event. For example, buttons will create a 'When this element is clicked...' event. If a workflow already exists, this takes you to the existing workflow in the Workflow Tab. To create a different type of event or create a second event, go to the Workflow Tab and click the 'Click here to add an event...' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#this-element-isnt-clickable-3)

This element isn't clickable

When checked, the element will not trigger a workflow when clicked nor will the user's cursor change to a pointer when they hover over the element. In addition, the item will display according to any specified conditional formatting for the 'isn't clickable' state. This can be used to stop a user from moving forward in a flow until some condition is true. For example, we might disable a specific action until the user is logged in. 'This input should not be empty' can also be used to prevent the user from clicking.

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#group-focus)

Group Focus


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Watch our Academy quick tip on how to set up a Group Focus

A group focus is a group that is automatically hidden when the user clicks outside of it. Position it relative to another element. This is especially useful for dropdown menus.

**Note:** Although the Group Focus container is fixed relative to the page, the iOS keyboard appear event can cause the Group Focus container to move to an unexpected location due to how Apple handles this event.

**Tip:** The same element can now be used to open and close a group focus element. Best practice would be to use a “Toggle an element” workflow action on the element to simply open & close the group focus.

**Note: I**f you have a searchbox within your Group Focus, clicking one of the searchbox's results will be considered clicking "outside" the Group Focus and therefore close it. A dropdown or custom searchbox are often used in these designs instead.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#reference-element)

Reference element

Specify an element to be the location reference for the group.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#offset-top)

Offset top

Pushes the group this number of pixels below the reference element.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#offset-left)

Offset left

Pushes the group this number of pixels left of the reference element.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#type-of-content-4)

Type of content

This allows you to select the type of thing this container displays. Most elements that have this property also have a Data source property, which lets you select the displayed thing or list of things. The value of the data source must match the selected type of content. Let's look at an example of a map with hotels marked. When you click a marker, a popup displays with the name of the hotel. The Type of content would be 'Hotel' and the Data source would be 'Map's current marker.'

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#data-source-4)

Data source

Define the thing or list of things that should be displayed in this container. For example, in groups or popups, only a single thing will display, but for repeating groups a list of things will display. Child elements can access the Data source from their parent. If the parent contains a list of things, the child has access to the individual list items. A common scenario for groups is to display something, like the current user, and then have the group contain the inputs that modify the user's properties, such as text and images that display information about the user. The Type of content determines what kind of thing you are allowed to set in the Data source field. If the type of thing does not match the Data source, it will be marked as an issue, and you must change one or the other to match. The Data source can come from anywhere in Bubble. For example, it could be the result of a search, a custom or built-in Data source, or it could be dynamic and come from the user. The Data source will update when its source changes. For examle, if the search result changes because a user modified some data used in the search constraints, the displayed list will automatically change. For additional flexibility, you can update the Data source in the Conditional Formatting Tab in the Property Editor. Finally, the Data source property can be modified by workflows. Changes made by actions override whatever you specify as the Data source directly or as a conditional state until you run a reset action, which then restores the original setting.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#collapse-this-elements-height-when-hidden-1)

Collapse this element's height when hidden

By default, hiding an element leaves a blank space on the page, exposing whatever is underneath it. When you select this option, however, the elements below the hidden element are pulled upward to fill the space.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#animate-the-collapse-operation-1)

Animate the collapse operation

This option is only visible if 'Collapse this element's height when hidden' is selected. By default, elements collapse and expand instantly, but this option animates with either Fade In/Out or Slide Up/Down.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#animation-style-1)

Animation style

This option allows you to choose whether an element's animation will Fade In/Out or Slide Up/Down.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#start-edit-workflow-4)

Start/Edit workflow

Click this button to create a new workflow triggered by this element. The type of event depends on the element and is generally the most common event. For example, buttons will create a 'When this element is clicked...' event. If a workflow already exists, this takes you to the existing workflow in the Workflow Tab. To create a different type of event or create a second event, go to the Workflow Tab and click the 'Click here to add an event...' button.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/core-resources/elements/containers-1#this-element-isnt-clickable-4)

This element isn't clickable

When checked, the element will not trigger a workflow when clicked nor will the user's cursor change to a pointer when they hover over the element. In addition, the item will display according to any specified conditional formatting for the 'isn't clickable' state. This can be used to stop a user from moving forward in a flow until some condition is true. For example, we might disable a specific action until the user is logged in. 'This input should not be empty' can also be used to prevent the user from clicking.

Last updated 3 years ago

Was this helpful?

# Installing and using Plugins | Bubble Docs

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/installing-and-using-plugins#installing-plugins)

Installing plugins


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You install and manage plugins in the _Plugins_ tab. This tab shows the installed plugins on the left, and details for each plugin on the right. At the top right, you'll find the 'Add Plugins' button that lets you search for plugins and install new ones. You can sort plugins by rating, usage or submission date.

Once you've installed a plugin, click DONE and you'll see the newly installed plugin in the Plugins tab.

### 

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/installing-and-using-plugins#billing)

Billing

When you subscribe to a plugin, you're not immediately billed. Your next monthly bill will contain a retroactive charge for any time on the previous billing period that was spent with the plugin in the app.

**Note:** If you are on a free trial and you add a plugin subscription, this will modify your subscription and you will get charged for that plugin & a month of the paid plan immediately. Consider waiting until after your free trial is over to add a paid plugin if this is undesirable.

If you choose to remove the plugin, you get credited for the remaining time, so you're only paying for the time that the plugin is **actually active** in your app. You can change to the one-time license fee at any time by cancelling your plugin subscription and purchasing the plugin license.

Plugins are subscribed to on the same billing frequency as your application's paid plan, so if you subscribe to a plugin while on an annual plan, you will also be subscribed to the plugin at its annual rate.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F34394582-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F-M5sbzwG7CljeZdkntrL%252Fuploads%252FPFxFoy7Xq5IH2QzTkp9e%252Fplugin-store%25402x.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dab4a44df-6e27-43d1-a698-577078687e27&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=ad9054a&sv=2)

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/installing-and-using-plugins#configuring-plugins)

Configuring plugins


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Many plugins, based on external services, require you to enter some keys to identify your app with them. You can enter these keys in the Plugins tab.

Keys are usually shared among the Development and Live versions of your app, but if you need to use different keys (for instance so that development sessions don't interfere with production analytics), you can enter a development version of your key. If a development key is provided this one will be used in the development version of your app, otherwise the live key will be used.

This view also shows the different items that the plugin is adding to your app. Elements, API calls and actions will be listed. In general, you'll find API calls in the API provider dropdown in the 'Get data from API' palette, you'll find elements in the New Element palette, and actions will be in the Plugins section of the New Action element.

Lastly, you can uninstall the plugin from your app. Note that adding plugins adds some code to your app and can make pages heavier and slow page loads down. Try to keep the list to the necessary plugins you need when running your app.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F34394582-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F-M5sbzwG7CljeZdkntrL%252Fuploads%252FItHMdCCVUE5v1vrn8KLs%252Fgoogle-places-bubble-plugn%25402x.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Ded90c127-080e-4440-b0e0-61612b899415&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=a600e935&sv=2)

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/installing-and-using-plugins#reviewing-plugins)

Reviewing plugins


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As a way to moderate the marketplace, ratings and users' feedback are very important. You can rate plugins in the App Editor (and please do so!). Ratings are communicated to the Plugin Builder via the Statistics tab in the Plugin Editor, and will be useful for them to improve the plugin.

![](https://manual.bubble.io/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F34394582-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F-M5sbzwG7CljeZdkntrL%252Fuploads%252FViXX968I4f9HLVeXiCkk%252Freview-bubble-plugin%25402x.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Db70aafbb-c81e-4717-a003-26147060c31d&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=11c08866&sv=2)

[](https://manual.bubble.io/help-guides/integrations/using-plugins/installing-and-using-plugins#getting-help)

Getting help


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As each plugin is different, we recommend using the support of the Community through the [forum](https://forum.bubble.is/)
 for help to use a plugin. You can also contact the plugin developer directly.

Last updated 2 years ago

Was this helpful?
